STATEMENT OF CONFIDENTIALITY All information in this document is <CLIENTNAME> PRIVILEGED and for INTERNAL USE ONLY. The information in this document may contain <CLIENTNAME> proprietary trade secrets and confidential information, the disclosure of which could substantially benefit competitors. As a result, this document should not be disclosed, used or duplicated – in whole or in part without the explicit authorization of <CLIENTNAME> management. The information is intended solely for the use of authorized recipients. If you are not the intended recipient, you are prohibited from reading, using, disseminating, distributing and copying this document. In accessing the document, the reader warrants that the information contained herein shall only be treated for internal use. No content of any nature shall be shared with any other external party or unauthorized recipient.

<CLIENTNAME> Information Risk Management Policy Classification: Internal 1. INTRODUCTION This Information Risk Management Policy (“Policy”) is a key component of <CLIENTNAME>’s overall Integrated Management System (IMS) and overarches the detailed risk management framework and procedure. Adherence to this Policy will help protect <CLIENTNAME> against known and unknown information security risks, whether internal or external, deliberate or accidental. <CLIENTNAME> recognizes that effective identification and management of risk is mandatory to the functioning of a robust Integrated Management System (IMS) comprising of Information Security Management System (ISMS), IT Service Management System (ITSMS), Business Continuity Management System (BCMS) and Privacy Information Management System (PIMS). This Policy will be reviewed at least annually or following any changes to contributing internal or external factors. 2. PURPOSE The purpose of this Policy is to ensure that information risks in the organization are identified, assessed, evaluated, monitored, reported and treated according to the business, security, privacy and continuity requirements and objectives of the organization. Further, the Policy establishes a commitment to comply with applicable sections of the federal laws and regulations, including but not limited to UAE Information Assurance Regulation (UAE IAR) and ISO 27001. The following are the key objectives of this Policy: 2.1 Establish an Information Risk Management Framework: Establishment of an organization-wide Information Security Risk Management Framework. 2.2 Address Information Security (IS) risks: Identification, analysis and evaluation of organizations IS risks to select appropriate IS risk treatment options. 3. SCOPE This Policy encompasses all system components that are owned, leased, rented, operated, maintained, and otherwise controlled by <CLIENTNAME> or a third-party on behalf of <CLIENTNAME>, and also applies to any system or system components, both internally and externally, that interact with these systems. This Policy applies to all employees and contractors who access or manage information and information assets or use the organization's information and communications technology (ICT) systems. Any exceptions on the applicability of this Policy should be formally requested through the Policy Exception Management procedure. Document No: <CLIENTNAME>-ORG-IMS-POL-026
<CLIENTNAME> Information Risk Management Policy Classification: Internal 4. POLICY STATEMENT <CLIENTNAME> shall implement an information risk management program that shall require conducting risk assessments, identifying information risks, treating the risks and continuously monitoring risks by evaluating the effectiveness of information security controls to ensure efficient risk handling. 5. POLICY DETAILS 5.1. Information Risk Management Framework 5.1.1 <CLIENTNAME> shall define an Information Risk Management (IRM) framework that provides a process to integrate security, privacy, and business continuity risk management activities into business processes. 5.1.2 The IRM framework shall consider business objectives and constraints placed by applicable laws, directives, executive orders, policies, standards, or regulations. 5.1.3 <CLIENTNAME>’s IRM framework shall include the following activities at a minimum: a. Prepare: Establish context by understanding mission, business objectives, and information systems to manage <CLIENTNAME>, its security, privacy, and business continuity risks. b. Identify: Provide a structured way to determine the criticality of the information assets at <CLIENTNAME>, and identify its associated risks. c. Assess: Analyze, evaluate and categorize risks based on its likelihood of materializing and its impacts to <CLIENTNAME>’s business and information systems. d. Select: Select, tailor and document the controls necessary to protect <CLIENTNAME> and its information systems commensurate with risks. e. Implement: Establish new or utilize existing processes, procedures, tools and technologies to meet the intent of selected controls. f. Review: Determine whether the selected controls are implemented correctly, operate as intended, and produce the desired outcomes to meet <CLIENTNAME>’s information security and data privacy requirements. g. Monitor: Maintain ongoing situational awareness about the security and privacy posture of <CLIENTNAME> and its information systems to support risk management decisions. 5.1.4 The IRM framework shall establish and implement a plan for communicating risk information and consulting key stakeholders, including decision-makers within the organization during all stages of the risk management process. 5.2. Information Risk Assessment 5.2.1 <CLIENTNAME> shall establish a risk assessment process to be used as the basis for the identification, definition and prioritization of risks. 5.2.2 The risk assessment process shall include at a minimum: a. Identification of the vulnerabilities that exist in the information assets. b. Identification of threats that may exploit a vulnerability. c. Qualitative analysis and evaluation of the impact to the confidentiality, integrity, availability, secrecy and privacy of information assets if a threat exploits vulnerabilities. Document No: <CLIENTNAME>-ORG-IMS-POL-026
<CLIENTNAME> Information Risk Management Policy Classification: Internal d. Identification and definition of measures and/or controls used to protect the confidentiality, integrity, availability, secrecy and privacy of information assets. 5.2.3 The risk assessment shall be performed/updated when environmental, operational or technical changes arise that impact <CLIENTNAME>’s business processes and information assets. Such changes include but are not limited to: a. New threats or risks with respect to the business processes and information assets. b. An information security incident. c. Outcomes of any internal or external assessments. d. Changes to information security requirements or responsibilities. (e.g., new federal or state law or regulation, new or modified security controls implemented, etc.). e. Changes to the <CLIENTNAME>’s organizational or technical infrastructure that impact information assets (e.g., addition of a new network, new hardware/software standard implemented, new method of creating, receiving, maintaining or transmitting data, etc.). 5.3. Information Risk Categorization 5.3.1 <CLIENTNAME> shall categorize risks based on business objectives, data sensitivity, threat exposure and vulnerabilities pursuant to information assets. 5.3.2 The categorization process shall take into account the probability and impact of risks materializing and affecting business objectives. 5.3.3 Risks shall be expressed qualitatively as per the risk categorizations defined under the Information Risk Management Framework. 5.3.4 Adequate resources to address risks shall be allocated according to the risk categorization. 5.4. Information Risk Treatment 5.4.1 <CLIENTNAME> shall select and implement risk treatment measures to reasonably protect the confidentiality, integrity, availability, secrecy and privacy of information assets. One or more of the following methods shall be used to manage risk: a. Risk Reduction: Reducing risk by applying security controls. b. Risk Avoidance: Avoiding the activity or condition causing the risk. c. Risk Transfer: Transferring the risk to another party. d. Risk Retention: Accepting the risk based on the organization’s risk acceptance criteria established in the Information Security Risk Management Framework. 5.4.2 <CLIENTNAME> shall document a risk treatment plan to outline the approach to be used to treat the risks. Such risk treatment plans shall include at a minimum: a. Description of information risks. b. Appropriate management actions. c. Responsibilities and priorities for managing information security risks (risk ownership). d. Target dates for implementation of the identified controls. 5.4.3 Risk treatment plans shall be monitored and reviewed periodically as deemed necessary to ensure that risk is managed on a continuous basis. Document No: <CLIENTNAME>-ORG-IMS-POL-026
<CLIENTNAME> Information Risk Management Policy Classification: Internal 5.5. Selection of Controls 5.5.1 <CLIENTNAME> shall select and implement appropriate controls to mitigate identified risks based on relevance, nature, feasibility and cost effectiveness of the controls. 5.5.2 Under the <CLIENTNAME> Integrated Management System (IMS), controls shall be selected from the list of following standards/frameworks/regulations, inclusive of but not limited to: a. ISO 27001. b. ISO 27701. c. ISO 22301. d. UAE Information Assurance (IA) Regulation. 5.6. Information Risk Monitoring 5.6.1 <CLIENTNAME> shall continuously review and monitor outcomes of risk assessment and treatment. The monitoring processes shall evaluate: a. The effectiveness of security controls. b. Changes to information assets and environments of operations. c. Compliance with federal and state laws and regulations and applicable standards. Document No: <CLIENTNAME>-ORG-IMS-POL-026

data.heading: The purpose of this Access Control - Full Document
data.passage_text:
INTRODUCTION
UAE Public Prosecution has defined access controls across its networks, information assets, Information Technology (IT) systems and services to provide authorized, granular, auditable, and appropriate user access, and to ensure appropriate preservation of data confidentiality, integrity, and availability.
A robust access control management system is paramount to ensure UAEPP users are appropriately identified and have the authorization to access information assets and IT resources.
PURPOSE
The purpose of this Access Control Policy is to ensure that access is controlled at the user, application, network, and operating system level as well as for mobile computing.
Further, this Policy establishes a commitment to comply with applicable sections of the federal and global laws and regulations, including but not limited to UAE Information Assurance Regulation, ISO/IEC 27001:2022.
The following are the key objectives of this Policy:
User Access Management: Establish and implement a formal user access management process for how user accounts and privileges are created, managed and deleted in line with organizational policies and security practices.
Monitoring and Review of User access rights:  Define the requirements for performing periodic review and monitoring of user access.
SCOPE
This Policy applies to all employees, contractors and third parties who access or manage information and information assets or use the organization's information and communications technology (ICT) systems.
POLICY STATEMENT
The organization shall develop, implement, and maintain an organization wide access control framework to:
Control access to information and information assets.
Manage registration and deregistration of user accounts/access.
Control access to both internal and external network services.
Control access to operating systems.
Control access to applications.
Ensure appropriate protection when accessing mobile computing and teleworking services.
Policy Details
Access Control
UAE Public Prosecution shall:
Ensure that asset owners determine appropriate access rules, privileges, and restrictions for specific user roles towards their assets.
Ensure that the following factors are considered while defining the access control framework:
Security requirements for individual business applications.
Information dissemination and authorization process, e.g., the need-to-know principle, information security levels and classification of information.
Consistency between the access rights and data classification levels for different systems and networks.
Relevant legislation and any contractual obligations regarding limitation of access to data or services.
Segregation of access control management roles, e.g., access request, access authorization, and access administration.
Requirements for formal authorization of access requests.
Requirements for periodic review of access rights.
Removal of access rights.
Logging and monitoring of records of all significant events concerning the use and management of user identities and security credentials.
Roles with privileged access.
Ensure that business information is not compromised while using mobile devices, e.g., notebooks, laptops, smart cards, and mobile phones by implementing appropriate security controls.
Implement security controls to protect mobile devices.
Establish rules on connecting mobile devices to networks and guidance on the use of these devices in public places.
Access to Network and Network Shares
UAE Public Prosecution shall:
Formulate a procedure to address security concerns in the use of network connections and network services including wireless network, VPN, and third-party/ external connections.
Ensure that the procedure on the use of network services is consistent with this Policy.
Wireless Access
UAE Public Prosecution shall:
Utilize authentication protocols which provide credential protection and mutual authentication for wireless networks.
Ensure the selection of appropriate controls for wireless networks to address security risks such as undetected interception.
Conduct periodic wireless surveys to determine security and quality of the wireless network.
User Registration and De-Registration
UAE Public Prosecution shall:
Ensure that the process of managing user ID’s include the following areas as a minimum:
Using unique and identifiable user ID’s to enable users to be linked to and held responsible for their actions.
The use of shared IDs is prohibited by default, unless there is a valid business justification which is approved by UAEPP’s senior management and CISO.
Immediately disable or remove user ID’s of users who have left the organization.
Perform periodic review of user access across the information and information assets to determine and disable redundant user ID’s and access privileges that are no longer required or authorized.
Ensure that unused/redundant user ID’s are not issued to other users.
The number of privileged user IDs shall be strictly limited to those individuals who shall have such privileges for authorized business purposes.
Multi-user systems administrators shall have at least two user-IDs to separate their privileged access from their ordinary day-to-day access.
User Access Provisioning
UAE Public Prosecution shall:
Ensure that the provisioning/deprovisioning process for assigning or revoking access rights granted to user IDs are approved, and in line with organizational policies and security practices.
The provisioning process for assigning or revoking access rights for users shall consider the followings:
Obtaining a proper authorization from the system or asset owner.
Segregation of duties to ensure a proper access level is given.
Access rights are not activated until an authorization process is completed.
Records reflecting all user access rights are centrally kept up-to-date.
Reviewing users access rights in a regular basis.
Management of Privileged Access Rights
UAE Public Prosecution shall:
Control the allocation of privileged access rights through a formal authorization process in accordance with the relevant access control procedures.
Consider the following steps, at a minimum, for managing privileged access rights:
Establish process to identify requirements for privileged access.
Privileged and service account access rights need to be allocated to users/applications on a need-to-use basis and on an event-by-event basis in line with the access control procedure.
Maintain an authorization process and a record of all privileges allocated.
Not grant privileged access rights until the authorization process is completed.
Assign privileged access rights to a user ID different from those used for regular business activities.
Perform regular review of user roles with privileged access rights to verify if they are in line with their duties.
Establish and maintain specific procedures in order to avoid the unauthorized use of generic administration user ID’s, according to systems’ configuration capabilities.
Prohibit sharing of passwords for generic administration of user ID’s. The use of shared IDs is prohibited by default, unless there is a valid business justification which is approved by UAEPP’s senior management and CISO.
Where possible, implement automated or manual controls to support privileged access management process.
Where utilities are used with privileged credentials, the utilities are to be secured in line with this Policy to prevent misuse/ alteration of business sensitive data.
Review of User Access Rights
UAE Public Prosecution shall:
Review users' access rights at regular intervals and after Promotion, Transfer, Demotion and/or Termination of employment.
Review authorizations for privileged access rights at more frequent intervals.
Restrict such privileges upon detection of any misconduct of privileged access rights.
User Security Credentials Management
UAE Public Prosecution shall:
Ensure that the users are required to sign a statement to keep security credentials confidential.
Configure information assets to implement prescribed controls on access management.
Establish user access and secure credentials management procedures.
Use of Security Credentials & UAEPP Password Policy
UAE Public Prosecution shall:
Enforce selection of quality passwords with sufficient minimum length when passwords are used as security credentials. At a minimum, passwords must meet the following requirements:
A minimum account lockout reset shall be set to fifteen (15) minutes when an account is locked due to an invalid attempt lockout.
Passwords shall not be transmitted in clear text during communications over networks or when stored.
Credentials shall not be hardcoded into applications.
The use of default credentials shall not be authorized.
Approved encryption or hashing techniques shall be utilized where credentials are stored.
Password shall not contain personal information that can be easily guessed by a malicious actor, such as user name, phone number, birthdays, etc.
Password shall not contain a complete dictionary word.
Password shall not contain common acronyms, common words or reverse spelling of words, word or number patterns.
Users shall use different passwords for General and Administrative accounts.
Passwords can be reset only following the authorized mechanisms, such as User self-service.
Where possible, utilize Single Sign-on capabilities for multiple services where the user is assured a reasonable level of protection in line with the Policy.
Develop application specific access and secure logon control procedures to ensure compliance to the Policy including usage of Multi Factor authentication checks (smart cards, tokens, biometric, etc.).
The users shall:
Keep authentication details confidential, ensuring that they are not divulged to any other parties, including people of authority.
Change security credentials whenever there is any indication of their possible compromise.
Not use the same security credentials for business and non-business purposes.
Secure Log-on
Login into operating systems shall be based on a formal secure logon procedure.
All systems shall display a general notice warning message that access to UAEPP's systems is granted to authorized users only.
The logon process on any system shall display only the limited information about the system and its purposed use.
When strong authentication and identification is required, authentication methods other than passwords (e.g., token IDs, smart cards or biometrics) shall be implemented.
All systems shall limit the number of unsuccessful logon attempts allowed; the following shall be considered:
Recording both successful and unsuccessful attempts.
Forcing a time delay before further logon attempts are allowed or rejecting any further attempts without specific authorization.
Sending an alarm message to the system console if the maximum number of logon attempts is reached.
ICT Administrators (e.g., system admin, application admin, DB admin and network admin) shall review all unsuccessful log attempts periodically.
Use of Privileged Utility Programs
System utilities shall be restricted from all users unless approved by CISO/ IS Team.
All access to system utilities shall be logged and reviewed by UAEPP IS Team.
Access to and use of system programs shall be restricted and controlled.
All unnecessary system utilities and software shall be removed.
Publicly Accessible Content
UAE Public Prosecution shall:
Define publishing guidelines with required reviews and approvals for any information to be publicly available.
Designate individuals who are authorized to review and post information onto a publicly accessible information system.
Provide training and awareness sessions for authorized individuals to ensure that publicly accessible information is sanitized from non-public information.
Periodically scan publicly accessible information for non-public information and correct any inconsistency.
ROLES AND RESPONSIBILITIES
Executive & Senior Management
Make available and support the required resources to drive the implementation of this Access Control Policy.
Where relevant, provide direction to and participate in reviews and oversight of this Access Control Policy.
Information Security Department
Establish and enforce this Policy that outlines the roles and responsibilities of different stakeholders and develop procedures to facilitate the associated access controls' implementation.
Support in the implementation of the controls outlined in this Policy.
Manage and document exceptions related to this Policy.
Establish access review procedure in line with this Policy.
Establish procedures for credential management.
Information Technology
Implement appropriate controls in line with this Policy to ensure access to information systems are secured and restricted to authorized personnel only.
Configure equipment and systems to implement automatic protections when left unattended.
Employees, Line Managers and Third Parties (Contractors, Vendors, etc.)
Employees and third parties who access organizational resources, information and information assets or use information and communications technology (ICT) systems of the organization are responsible for adhering to this Policy and all its applicable procedures.
Restrict access to confidential files on a need-to-know basis.
Report potential violations of this Policy to the UAEPP IS Team.
Ensure due diligence while approving exception requests for their direct reports.
POLICY ENFORCEMENT AND EXCEPTIONS
This Policy is to be part of all employees, contractors and third parties’ processes, and all are responsible for understanding and adhering to this Policy. Any violation of this Policy shall be reported to Policy Owner. Violation of this Policy shall be subjected to disciplinary action by the UAE Public Prosecution's Human Resources, taking into consideration the recommendation of the Information Security department and in accordance with other applicable policies, laws and regulations.
Disciplinary action may range from a verbal warning to discontinuation of access to facility or services, or termination of employment/contract/agreement, and possible legal action. The actual action taken shall depend on factors such as the seriousness of the breach and the user's disciplinary record.
Any exceptions on the applicability of this Policy should be formally requested through the ‘Policy Exception Management procedure.’
MONITORING AND REVIEW
This Policy is developed and owned by the UAE Public Prosecution Information Security (IS) Team. It is reviewed by the CISO and approved by the Head of UAE Public Prosecution.
UAE Public Prosecution IS Team will initiate a review and of this Policy annually or whenever a significant change occurs in the organization's operating environment.
Feedback, suggestions or questions regarding the Policy shall be directed to the UAE Public Prosecution IS Team at infosec@pp.gov.ae.

INTRODUCTION
<CLIENT> (hereon referred to as <CLIENT>) has embarked on a robust Asset Management initiative to safeguard information assets collected and generated during the normal course of its business operations. As a company that delivers cybersecurity services, <CLIENT> maintains and uses valuable assets. As such, a robust and comprehensive Asset Management program is required to ensure that the assets maintained and operated by <CLIENT> are secured and protected throughout the lifecycle.
<CLIENT> is responsible for identifying assets and how they are used within the company; therefore, it shall set policies, processes, and standard operating procedures for handling assets, implement appropriate technical controls, and educate users about current threats to the assets that they work with and observe best practices for asset protection.
<CLIENT> employees are expected to understand and comply with this Policy and ultimately be responsible for protecting all Information Assets from unauthorized access. Compliance with this Policy is highly important to safeguard the organization's assets and reputation. Any Policy violation may invite appropriate disciplinary action per the corporate policies, applicable laws and regulations in the United Arab Emirates.
PURPOSE
This Asset Management Policy ("Policy") aims to ensure appropriate classification and protection of <CLIENT>'s information and assets.
Further, the Policy establishes a commitment to comply with applicable sections of the federal and global laws and regulations, including but not limited to UAE Information Assurance Regulation, Federal Decree Law No. 45 of 2021 regarding the Protection of Personal Data, ISO 27001, ISO 27701, ISO 20000, ISO 22301 and UAE National Cyber Risk Management Framework (NCRMF).
The following are the key objectives of this Policy:
Establish asset register: Development and maintenance of an asset register.
Handling of information: Correct and consistent handling of information assets in accordance with the organization’s information classification scheme.
Management of media: Correct and consistent management of the asset's lifecycle.
SCOPE
This Policy applies to all employees and critical third parties who access or manage information and information assets or use the organization’s information and communications technology (ICT) systems.
Any exceptions on the applicability of this Policy should be formally requested through the ‘Policy Exception Management procedure’.
POLICY STATEMENT
<CLIENT> shall ensure the security of assets by:
Identifying information assets.
Ensuring that a current and accurate inventory of assets is developed and maintained through an asset register to include responsibilities and classification based on Data Security Policy (<CLIENT>-ORG-IMS-POL-003).
POLICY DETAILS
Asset Inventory
<CLIENT> shall:
Identify assets relevant in the information lifecycle (creation, processing, storage, transmission, deletion, destruction, and protection) and document their importance.
Ensure that all organizational assets are considered for the asset management process using a centralized and efficient approach.
Ensure that documentation is done in dedicated asset inventories as appropriate. The asset inventory at a minimum shall contain:
Type of asset.
Asset ownership.
Location.
Backup information.
Related licenses.
Asset importance/criticality.
Asset Ownership
<CLIENT> shall:
Establish and implement a process to ensure timely assignment of asset ownership.
Assign/reassign ownership when assets are created or transferred.
Assign ownership to individuals having approved management responsibility across the asset lifecycle. Asset owners shall have the following responsibilities at a minimum:
Perform inventory of the applicable assets.
Classify and protect the assets.
Define and periodically review access restrictions and classifications to important assets, considering applicable access control policies in accordance with <CLIENT> Access Control Policy (<CLIENT>-ORG-IMS-POL-017).
Asset Labelling
<CLIENT> shall:
Label information assets in physical and electronic formats according to the established information classification scheme as per the <CLIENT> Data Security Policy (<CLIENT>-ORG-IMS-POL-003).
Develop detailed procedures to give guidance on where and how labels are attached in consideration of how the information is accessed or the assets are handled depending on the types of media.
Ensure that output from systems carry an appropriate classification label aligned to <CLIENT> Data Security Policy (<CLIENT>-ORG-IMS-POL-003).
Ensure labels are easily recognizable and are included in emails, header, and footer of manually created or system-generated documents.
Ensure appropriate awareness is provided to employees and third-party users regarding information labelling procedures.
Asset Handling
<CLIENT> shall:
Develop procedures for handling, processing, storing, and communicating information consistent with its classification level. The following items should be considered, at a minimum:
Access restrictions supporting the protection requirements for each level of classification.
Protection of temporary or permanent copies of information to a level consistent with the protection of the original information.
Storage of IT assets in accordance with manufacturers' specifications.
Safeguard information in accordance with the established procedures.
Ensure appropriate awareness is provided to employees and third-party users regarding the information handling procedures.
Removable Media Management
<CLIENT> shall:
Ensure that all information is stored only in organization authorized media.
Ensure that contents of any re-usable media that are to be removed from the organization is made unrecoverable when no longer required.
Ensure that appropriate authorization is obtained for media removed from the organization and a record of such removals is kept in order to maintain an audit trail.
Store all media in a safe and secure environment in accordance with manufacturers' specifications.
Apply cryptographic techniques to protect data on removable media.
Ensure that all removable media is scanned for malware before utilization.
Ensure transfer of data to fresh media before it gets unreadable.
Securely store/backup multiple copies of valuable data on separate media to further reduce the risk of incidental and/or accidental data damage or loss.
Restrict and monitor the usage of removable media to limit the opportunity for data loss.
Ensure that any organization-controlled media with data is not sent to the manufacturer for any repair purposes to avoid leakage of residual confidential information.
Disposal of Media
<CLIENT> shall:
Establish procedures for secure disposal of media containing organizational information based on the sensitivity of that information.
Ensure that the procedures are defined to identify media devices that might require secure disposal.
Ensure that media, both paper and digital, are destroyed when no longer serving the organization.
Ensure that records of disposed media (containing or used to contain confidential information) are kept when no longer needed.
Physical Media Transfer
<CLIENT> shall:
Utilize reliable transport or couriers for transportation of physical media between sites.
Develop a list of authorized couriers that are approved by the management.
Define labelling requirements for physical media carrying sensitive information.
Ensure that logs of the transfer are kept identifying the content of the media, protection applied, time(s) of transfer to the transit custodian(s), and the receipt at destination.
Consider measures to be taken in the event of loss of physical media in transit carrying sensitive information.
Acceptable Use of Assets
<CLIENT> shall:
Ensure that usage of all organization’s information, information assets and information and communications technology (ICT) systems are approved by the information security department prior to deployment.
Establish, document, and circulate the rules for the acceptable use of assets in line with the <CLIENT> Acceptable Use Policy (<CLIENT>-ORG-IMS-POL-002) and disciplinary procedures.
Consider the information provided by other members of an information sharing community as an asset and protect and disseminate in accordance with agreed upon rules.
Bring Your Own Device (BYOD) Arrangements
<CLIENT> shall:
Provide means to securely access organizational information and information assets via ‘Bring Your Own Device’ (BYOD).
Establish and implement information security requirements to secure organizational information and information assets via ‘Bring Your Own Device’ (BYOD) in line with <CLIENT> Bring Your Own Device (BYOD) Policy (<CLIENT>-ORG-IMS-POL-020).
ROLES AND RESPONSIBILITIES
POLICY ENFORCEMENT AND EXCEPTIONS
This Policy is to be part of all Employees' and Third Parties' processes, and all are responsible for understanding and adhering to this Policy. Any violation of this Policy shall be reported to Policy Owner. Violation of this Policy shall be subjected to disciplinary action by the <CLIENT>'s Human Capital Department, taking into consideration the recommendation of the Information Security department and in accordance with other applicable policies, laws and regulations.
Disciplinary action may range from a verbal warning to discontinuation of access to facility or services, or termination of employment/contract/agreement, and possible legal action. The actual action taken shall depend on factors such as the seriousness of the breach and the user's disciplinary record.
Any exceptions on the applicability of this Policy should be formally requested through the Policy Exception Management procedure.
MONITORING AND REVIEW
This Policy is developed and owned by the <CLIENT> Information Security (IS) Team. It is reviewed by the CISO and approved by the CEO.
<CLIENT> IS Team will initiate a review and of this Policy annually or whenever a significant change occurs in the organization’s operating environment.
Feedback, suggestions or questions regarding the Policy shall be directed to the <CLIENT> IS Team at infosec@<CLIENT>.com.

data.passage_id: clientname-IS-POL-00-Network and Communications Security Policy v1.2 2_1
data.heading: The purpose of this Network and Communications Security - Full Document
data.passage_text:
INTRODUCTION
Network and Communications Security aims to protect networks and communications from breaches, intrusions and other threats. It is vital that <CLIENTNAME> has a highly robust network and communication security in place at all times.
PURPOSE
The purpose of this Network and Communications Security Policy (“Policy”) is to ensure protection of information being exchanged within the organization and outside the organization.
Furthermore, the Policy establishes a commitment to comply with applicable sections of the federal and global laws and regulations, including but not limited to UAE Information Assurance Regulation, ISO 27001, ISO 27701 and National Cyber Risk Management Framework (NCRMF).
The following are the key objectives of this Policy:
Network protection: Ensuring all networks are adequately managed, controlled, and protected.
Information protection: Protect information involved in electronic messaging.
Secure Information Exchange: Establish requirements for the exchange of information and software between organization and external parties.
SCOPE
This Policy applies to all employees, contractors and third parties who access or manage information and information assets or use the organization's information and communications technology (ICT) systems.
Any exceptions on the applicability of this Policy should be formally requested through the ‘Policy Exception Management procedure.’
POLICY STATEMENT
<CLIENTNAME> shall ensure the protection of information transferred within the organization as well as information transferred with any external entity. Additionally, <CLIENTNAME> shall ensure that all networks are adequately protected at all times.
POLICY DETAILS
Network Controls
<CLIENTNAME> shall:
Establish the network and communications design based on the following minimum requirements:
Classification of the information transported and accessed across networks.
Risk assessment of threats and vulnerabilities to/of the network.
Level of trust between all components and organizations connected to the network.
Degree of availability and resilience required from the network.
Geographical topography of the network.
Security controls in place at locations from where the network shall be accessed.
Establish specific controls to ensure the security of internal networks and the protection of connected services from unauthorized access.
Establish specific controls to safeguard the confidentiality, availability and integrity of data passing over public networks or over wireless networks and protect the connected systems and applications.
Apply appropriate logging and monitoring controls for the network services to enable recording and detection of actions that may affect, or are relevant to, information security through use of centralised services.
Establish responsibilities and procedures for networking equipment management.
Ensure systems connected to the network are authenticated and restricted.
Implement communications and network management protocols that provide secure authentication and compliant transport encryption.
Patch and update network systems in accordance with <CLIENTNAME>’s Patch Management procedure.
Implement network and communication systems baseline security configuration requirements.
Backup network and communication systems configuration data in accordance with <CLIENTNAME>’s Backup & Restoration process.
Security of Network Services
<CLIENTNAME> shall:
Identify and implement the security arrangements necessary for security features, service levels, and management requirements.
Determine and regularly monitor the ability of the network service provider to manage agreed services in a secure manner.
Ensure that the right to audit clauses are agreed upon with all the network service providers.
Segregation in Networks
<CLIENTNAME> shall:
Manage security of large networks within the organization by segregating them into separate network zones.
Segregate networks considering sensitivity of information processed, access requirements, relative cost, and performance impact of incorporating suitable technology.
Implement routing controls shall such that access to applications or services is restricted to provide isolation between systems and network zones created as above.
Periodically evaluate the effectiveness of implemented segregation strategies and identify areas for improvement.
Security of Wireless Networks
<CLIENTNAME> shall:
Perform a risk assessment for wireless networks to identify security controls (e.g., strong authentication, cryptographic methods, and frequency selection) required to maintain network segregation.
Segregate wireless networks from internal and private networks and guest networks as required.
Restrict the use of 'guest' wireless networks to authorized guests.
Prohibit creation and use of ad-hoc wireless networks, including connecting of unapproved wireless base-stations to organization's core data network.
Implement mechanisms to ensure identification and isolation of rogue wireless access points.
Periodically evaluate the effectiveness of wireless security strategies and identify areas for improvement.
Information Transfer Security Procedures
<CLIENTNAME> shall:
Establish information transfer security procedures.
Ensure that the information transfer security procedure at a minimum, includes the following:
Use cases and appropriate security controls to be put in place ensures information is adequately protected during transfer.
Actions to be taken when issues arise regarding the transfer of information.
Ensure that the procedures and controls implemented for securing communication systems during information transfer consider the following items:
Protect transferred information from interception, copying, modification, misrouting, and destruction using cryptographic techniques.
Detect and protect against malware.
Develop guidelines outlining acceptable use of communication systems.
Develop retention and disposal guidelines for all business correspondence, including messages, in accordance with relevant national and local legislation and regulations.
Develop guidelines on using external public services such as instant messaging or file sharing for sharing company information.
Ensure that information transfer services comply with any relevant legal requirements.
Agreements on Information Transfer
<CLIENTNAME> shall:
Establish exchange agreements for information considered as confidential or sensitive while engaging with third parties to extend security controls to protect confidentiality, integrity, availability, secrecy, and privacy.
Include the following security conditions at a minimum, when establishing exchange agreements:
Management responsibilities for controlling and notifying transmission, dispatch, and receipt.
Procedures to ensure traceability and non-repudiation.
Minimum technical standards for packaging and transmission.
Escrow agreements (e.g.: if Source code is no longer available).
Responsibilities and liabilities in the event of information security incidents, such as loss of data.
Use of an agreed labelling system for sensitive or critical information, ensuring that the meaning of the labels is immediately understood, and that the information is appropriately protected.
Technical standards for recording and reading information and software.
Any special controls that are required to protect sensitive items, such as cryptography.
Acceptable levels of access control.
Establish and maintain procedures and standards to protect information and physical media in transit, and reference them in such exchange agreements.
Reflect the sensitivity of the business information involved in the security content of any agreement.
Electronic Messaging
<CLIENTNAME> shall:
Identify all means of electronic messaging for transmitted information assets.
Include the following security considerations at a minimum, for electronic messaging:
Protecting messages from unauthorized access, modification, or denial of service.
Ensuring correct addressing and transportation of the message.
General reliability and availability of the service.
Legal considerations, for example requirements for electronic signatures.
Stronger levels of authentication controlling access from publicly accessible networks.
Develop the capability to monitor electronic messaging to ensure implementation of controls and adherence of rules.
Establish capabilities to detect malicious contents in all types of electronic messaging.
Take necessary actions for incoming and outgoing email communications to prevent transmission of malicious or fraudulent content.
Confidentiality or Non-disclosure Agreements
<CLIENTNAME> shall:
Enforce confidentiality or non-disclosure agreements when sensitive information is exchanged, for external parties where contracts and other legal agreements are not in place.
Where external parties have been engaged contractually, mandate inclusion of the confidentiality or non-disclosure agreement as part of the contract.
Ensure that the confidentiality or non-disclosure agreements address the requirement to protect confidential information using legally enforceable terms.
Consider the following elements at a minimum, to identify the requirements for confidentiality or non-disclosure agreements:
A definition of the information to be protected (e.g., confidential information).
Expected duration of an agreement, including cases where confidentiality might need to be maintained indefinitely.
Required actions when an agreement is terminated.
Responsibilities and actions of signatories to avoid unauthorized information disclosure (such as “need to know”).
Ownership of information, trade secrets and intellectual property, and how this relates to the protection of confidential information.
The permitted use of confidential information, and rights of the signatory to use information.
The right to audit and monitor activities that involve confidential information.
Process for notification and reporting of unauthorized disclosure or confidential information breaches.
Terms for information to be returned or destroyed at agreement cessation.
Expected actions to be taken in case of a breach of this agreement.
Ensure confidentiality and non-disclosure agreements comply with all applicable laws and regulations.
Review the requirements for confidentiality and non-disclosure agreements periodically or when changes occur that influence these requirements.
Information Released into Information Sharing Communities
<CLIENTNAME> shall:
As applicable, ensure that all the information released into information sharing communities have valid justification and appropriate approval.
As applicable, implement technical controls to provide authenticity without compromising anonymity. For e.g., utilize controls to confirm that a communication originated from a member of the community without revealing the actual identity of the originator.
As applicable, ensure that each recipient is responsible for obtaining any necessary authorizations for wider release from the originator prior to onwards distribution of information.
As applicable, ensure all applicable information sharing communities have defined rules for the protection of information in transit, and only permit members to join the community if such rules are accepted and implemented by the prospective member.
As applicable, establish alternative mechanisms (when the primary mechanism is unavailable or insecure) for information sharing that do not rely on electronic messaging.
ROLES AND RESPONSIBILITIES
POLICY ENFORCEMENT AND EXCEPTIONS
This Policy is to be part of all employees, contractors and third parties' processes, and all are responsible for understanding and adhering to this Policy. Any violation of this Policy shall be reported to Policy Owner. Violation of this Policy shall be subjected to disciplinary action by the <CLIENTNAME>'s Human Capital Department, taking into consideration the recommendation of the Information Security department and in accordance with other applicable policies, laws and regulations.
Disciplinary action may range from a verbal warning to discontinuation of access to facility or services, or termination of employment/contract/agreement, and possible legal action. The actual action taken shall depend on factors such as the seriousness of the breach and the user's disciplinary record.
Any exceptions on the applicability of this Policy should be formally requested through the ‘Policy Exception Management procedure.’
MONITORING AND REVIEW
This Policy is developed and owned by the <CLIENTNAME> Information Security (IS) Team. It is reviewed by the CISO and approved by the CEO.
<CLIENTNAME> IS Team will initiate a review of this Policy annually or whenever a significant change occurs in the organization's operating environment.
Feedback, suggestions or questions regarding the Policy shall be directed to the <CLIENTNAME> IS Team at infosec@<clientname>.net.

data.passage_id: clientname-IS-POL-00-Information Systems Acquisition Development and Maintenance Policy 2_1
data.heading: This Information Systems Acquisition, Development and Maintenance - Full Document
data.passage_text:
INTRODUCTION
This Information Systems Acquisition, Development and Maintenance Policy outlines the framework for implementing appropriate security controls to ensure that day-to-day acquisition, development and maintenance of <CLIENT>’s Information systems are carried out in a controlled and secure manner.
PURPOSE
The purpose of this Information Systems Acquisition, Development and Maintenance Policy (“Policy”) is to define a framework for the Information System Acquisition, Development, and Maintenance process. This Policy shall ensure information assets are safeguarded against internal, external, deliberate, or accidental threats throughout their lifecycle.
Further, the Policy establishes a commitment to comply with applicable sections of the federal and global laws and regulations, including but not limited to UAE Information Assurance Regulation, ISO 27001 and UAE National Cyber Risk Management Framework (NCRMF).
The following are the key objectives of this Policy:
Definition of security requirements for information systems: Development of information security requirements for new information systems or enhancements to existing information systems.
Protection of information: Protection of information involved in application service transactions against incomplete transmission, misrouting, unauthorized message alteration, unauthorized disclosure, unauthorized message duplication, or replay.
SCOPE
This Policy applies to all employees, contractors and third parties who access or manage information and information assets or use the organization’s information and communications technology (ICT) systems.
Any exceptions on the applicability of this Policy should be formally requested through the ‘Policy Exception Management procedure.’
POLICY STATEMENT
<CLIENT> shall develop, implement, and maintain Information Systems Acquisition, Development and Maintenance Procedure to:
Provide strategies, tools, and techniques to protect risks towards supply chain.
Prevent unauthorized modification or misuse of the information in applications.
Maintain security in development and support processes.
Manage technical vulnerabilities.
POLICY DETAILS
Information Security Requirements Analysis and Specification
<CLIENT> shall:
Identify, document and review information security (IS) requirements using various methods such as deriving compliance requirements, threat modelling, incident reviews and/or use of vulnerability thresholds.
Ensure that the IS requirements and measures reflect the business value and potential adverse business impact resulting from lack of adequate security.
Identify and manage IS requirements and associated processes to ensure that they are integrated with the early stages of information systems projects.
Establish a formal risk-based security testing and acquisition process if products are acquired.
Ensure that the contracts with the supplier addresses the new risks introduced and the associated mitigation measures, before acquiring the product/service.
Evaluate and implement the available guidance for the product's security configuration and align with that organization’s final software/service stack.
Review and disable additional functionalities not used in the product to ensure that it does not introduce unacceptable additional risks.
Information Security Requirements during Acquisition and Development
<CLIENT> shall:
Define a process / criterion for addressing information security requirements during acquisition and/or development of information system(s).
Evaluate information security criteria prior to acquisition and/or development of an information system(s).
Conduct information security risk assessment and provide approval prior to purchase acquisition and/or development of an information system(s).
Define and establish contractual requirements with the third-parties addressing information security, confidentiality, non-disclosure, business continuity and privacy requirements.
Embed security by design into all architecture layers such as business, data, applications and technology by balancing the needs for information security with usability.
Secure the development environment according to the level of sensitivity, value and criticality of the information processed.
Restrict modifications to vendor proprietary software packages and limit the changes to such software through a strict change control and appropriate approvals. Where possible, request the vendors to perform such modifications.
Privacy by Design
<CLIENT> shall:
Define appropriate technical and organizational measures which are designed:
To implement the Data Protection Principles in an effective manner, and
To integrate into the processing of personal data, safeguards necessary for that purpose.
Identify the available technical and organizational measures, the cost of implementation and the nature, scope, context and purposes of processing personal data, as well as the risks of varying likelihood and severity for rights and freedoms of individuals presented by the processing of their personal data. If it is considered that the processing presents a high risk to individuals, a Data Protection Impact Assessment (DPIA) must be carried out in accordance with organization’s Data Protection Policy (<CLIENT>-ORG-IMS-POL-012).
Apply appropriate technical and organizational measures to ensure that, by default, only the personal data which is necessary for each processing activity is used, in relation to:
The amount of personal data collected;
The extent of processing that personal data;
The period of its storage; and
Its accessibility.
By default, restrict sharing personal data on a need-to-know basis.
Securing Application Services
<CLIENT> shall:
Evaluate and implement the security considerations for application services passing over public networks.
Ensure that the application service arrangements between partners are supported by a documented agreement that commits both parties to the agreed terms of services, including details of authorization.
Implement resilience requirements against attacks, including requirements for protecting the involved application servers or ensuring the availability of network interconnections required to deliver the service.
Evaluate and implement security considerations for protecting online transactions.
Ensure that the storage of the transaction details is located outside of any public access environment, e.g., on a storage platform existing on the entity's Intranet, and not retained and exposed on a storage medium directly accessible from the Internet.
Ensure that security is integrated and embedded throughout the entire end-to-end certificate/signature management process where a trusted authority is used (e.g., to issue and maintain digital signatures and/or digital certificates).
Ensure that adequate controls are in place to validate data being inputted into the new applications/ systems.
Implement a controlled central storage and control access for program source code in program source libraries.
Training and Awareness
<CLIENT> shall:
Ensure that appropriate training is provided to personnel to maintain the effectiveness of the security and privacy controls implemented within organizational systems.
Determine the specific training type necessary for different personnel and consider providing different training types for additional security and privacy functions, controls, and mechanisms.
Provide training, education, and awareness programs for organizational personnel regarding supply chain risk, available mitigation strategies, and when they should be used.
Protection of Test Data
<CLIENT> shall:
Avoid using operational data containing personally identifiable information or any other confidential information for testing purposes.
Protect all sensitive details and content by removal or modification when personally identifiable information or otherwise confidential information is used for testing purposes.
Apply the security measures and obtain necessary approvals, at a minimum, to protect operational data when used for testing purposes.
Supply Chain Protection Strategy
<CLIENT> shall:
Introduce security requirements into System Development Life Cycle (SDLC) as part of the supplier selection and acquisition strategy.
Perform a supply chain risk assessment (including sub-contractors) to identify risk and appropriate protection strategies.
Establish methods for reviewing and protecting development plans, evidence, and documentation commensurate with the information system's security category or classification level.
Ensure that contracts specify documentation protection requirements.
Reliable Delivery
<CLIENT> shall:
Employ tools and techniques to determine authenticity and completeness of procured hardware/ application.
Deploy configuration management tools, techniques, and measures to maintain provenance.
Utilize the risk-based approach to prioritize and address weaknesses or deficiencies identified during independent or organizational assessments of supply chain elements (e.g., penetration testing, audits, verification/validation activities).
Ensure appropriate security for supply chain elements or system components containing programmable logic that are critically important to system functions.
ROLES AND RESPONSIBILITIES
POLICY ENFORCEMENT AND EXCEPTIONS
This Policy is to be part of all Employees' and Third Parties' processes, and all are responsible for understanding and adhering to this Policy. Any violation of this Policy shall be reported to Policy Owner. Violation of this Policy shall be subjected to disciplinary action by the <CLIENT>'s Human Capital Department, taking into consideration the recommendation of the Information Security department and in accordance with other applicable policies, laws and regulations.
Disciplinary action may range from a verbal warning to discontinuation of access to facility or services, or termination of employment/contract/agreement, and possible legal action. The actual action taken shall depend on factors such as the seriousness of the breach and the user's disciplinary record. Any exceptions on the applicability of this Policy should be formally requested through the ‘Policy Exception Management procedure.’
MONITORING AND REVIEW
This Policy is developed and owned by the <CLIENT> Information Security (IS) Team. It is reviewed by the CISO and approved by the CEO.
<CLIENT> IS Team will initiate a review and of this Policy annually or whenever a significant change occurs in the organization’s operating environment.
Feedback, suggestions or questions regarding the Policy shall be directed to the <CLIENT> IS Team at infosec@<CLIENT>.net.

data.heading: The <CLIENTNAME> Vulnerability Management - Full Document
data.passage_text:
INTRODUCTION
The <CLIENTNAME> Vulnerability Management Policy (“Policy”) facilitates the cyclical practice of identifying, classifying, remediating, and mitigating IT related vulnerabilities, which may exist in <CLIENTNAME> information systems.
PURPOSE
The purpose of this Vulnerability Management Policy (“Policy”) is to establish controls to manage vulnerabilities effectively and in a timely manner which is required to maintain high levels of system and application security in a diverse IT environment.
SCOPE
The Policy covers all information systems owned, utilized and managed by <CLIENTNAME> including but not limited to:
Information systems owned by <CLIENTNAME>.
Hardware and network components such as routers, firewalls, switches, access points and other network infrastructure.
Operating Systems.
Applications.
Databases.
Servers.
POLICY STATEMENT
<CLIENTNAME> shall establish vulnerability management practices in order to proactively prevent the exploitation of vulnerabilities and potential loss of sensitive data. <CLIENTNAME> shall create and document systematic and accountable practices to maintain control over programs and applications, to evaluate installed and new devices and systems for vulnerabilities, and to mitigate other technical and non-technical vulnerabilities. The goals of this effort are to implement stronger protection for <CLIENTNAME> IT resources, ensure compliance with best practices, and reduce the impact of threats to <CLIENTNAME> and its constituents.
POLICY DETAILS
Vulnerability Management
<CLIENTNAME> shall:
Conduct annual vulnerability assessments and penetration testing and quarterly internal vulnerability assessments scans on information systems on a continuous basis. In addition, ad-hoc vulnerability assessments and penetration testing shall be conducted based upon specific requests from Senior management or Business units.
<CLIENTNAME> shall implement a patch management process to be carried out periodically on all critical infrastructure systems.
<CLIENTNAME> shall remediate all critical and high vulnerabilities upon performing impact analysis with IS team.
<CLIENTNAME> shall remediate all critical and high potential risk within 45 days based on impact analysis with IS team
Shall report periodic progress on the status of remediation shall be submitted upon request from <CLIENTNAME> IS team.
Update the security baseline documents periodically to address the latest vulnerabilities/configuration.
An assessment report containing the details of impacted assets, vulnerability description, severity and remediation measures shall be prepared and shared with relevant stakeholders of <CLIENTNAME> IS Team.
Remediation activities shall be prioritized based on severity level of the vulnerability and criticality of impacted asset.
Remediation activities shall be conducted as per the defined timeline. Any deviations from the defined timeline shall be endorsed by the Information Asset Owner and approved by the <CLIENTNAME> IS Team.
A remediation plan shall be developed, consisting of details including but not limited to:
Remediation activities owner.
Target remediation timeline.
Planned remediation activities.
Remediation activities shall comply with <CLIENTNAME> processes including but not limited to change management, asset management, service management, etc.
Remediation activities may include but are not limited to:
Deployment of a software or system patch.
Release of new non-vulnerable software, system or firmware.
Removal of vulnerable software or system component.
Configuration changes.
User awareness and training.
Remediation activities that involve configuration changes or application of patches shall be initiated only after appropriate security, quality, and compatibility testing is done.
Data and configuration backups shall be taken before initiating remediation activities.
Post implementation review shall be conducted for connected information systems’ configuration and functionality validation.
Status of open vulnerabilities shall be monitored on a continuous basis.
ROLES AND RESPONSIBILITIES
POLICY ENFORCEMENT AND EXCEPTIONS
Any violation of this Policy shall be reported to Policy Owner. Violation of this Policy shall be subjected to disciplinary action by the <CLIENTNAME>'s Human Capital Department, taking into consideration the recommendation of IS Team and in accordance with other applicable policies, laws, and regulations.
Disciplinary action may range from a verbal warning to discontinuation of access to facility or services, or termination of employment/contract/agreement, and possible legal action. The actual action taken shall depend on factors such as the seriousness of the breach and the user's disciplinary record.
Any exceptions on the applicability of this Policy should be formally requested through the ‘Policy Exception Management procedure’.
MONITORING AND REVIEW
This Policy is developed and owned by the <CLIENTNAME> IS Team. It is reviewed by the CISO and approved by the CEO.
<CLIENTNAME> IS Team shall initiate a review of this Policy annually or whenever a significant change occurs in the organization's operating environment.
Feedback, suggestions, or questions regarding the Policy shall be directed to the <CLIENTNAME> IS Team at infosec@<clientname>.net.

data.passage_id: clientname-IS-POL-00-Security Awareness and Training Policy v2.0 UAE IA Mapping_1
data.heading: The purpose of this Security Awareness and Training - Full Document
data.passage_text:
INTRODUCTION
<<CLIENT>> recognizes the importance of providing its employees/ end-users with appropriate awareness and training on information security, business continuity and data privacy requirements to protect confidentiality, integrity, availability, privacy and resiliency of its information and communications technology (ICT) systems. This is to protect information assets, resources and systems. The success of <<CLIENT>>’s awareness and training program, and the overall awareness of secure business practices, depends upon the awareness, behavior and culture of the organization.
PURPOSE
The purpose of this Security Awareness and Training Policy (“Policy”) is to define a framework for security awareness and training processes to ensure that a proactive and risk-aware information security culture exists within <<CLIENT>>.
Further, this Policy establishes a commitment to comply with applicable sections of the federal and global laws and regulations, including but not limited to UAE Information Assurance Regulation, UAE Federal Law 45, ISO 20000, ISO 27001, ISO 22301, and ISO 27701.
The following are the key objectives of this Policy:
Security Awareness and Training Program: Develop and implement organization wide awareness and training program through identification of information security training needs across awareness, behavior and culture.
Information Security Training: Educate all end-users in <<CLIENT>> to follow information security best practices to ensure protection of confidentiality, integrity, availability, privacy and resiliency of information assets.
Measurement and Evaluation: Establish measurements and evaluation methods to determine effectiveness of the security awareness and training program.
Awareness Campaign: Establish an awareness campaign in order to ensure a risk-aware information security culture.
SCOPE
This Policy applies to all employees, contractors and third parties who access or manage information and information assets or use the organization's information and communications technology (ICT) systems.
Any exceptions on the applicability of this Policy should be formally requested through the ‘Policy Exception Management procedure’.
POLICY STATEMENT
<<CLIENT>> shall develop, implement, and maintain an awareness and training program to inform all employees of the information security, business continuity and data privacy risks associated with their activities.
Further, <<CLIENT>> shall ensure that employees and third parties are adequately trained to carry out their assigned information security responsibilities.
Awareness and Training
Awareness and Training Program
<<CLIENT>> shall:
Develop and formalize an Information Security (IS) awareness and training program that ensures continued awareness and competence of all <<CLIENT>> employees, including contractors, third-parties, and all other stakeholders with access to information assets.
Ensure that Information Security awareness and training program raise awareness and compliance about <<CLIENT>>’s Integrated Management System and its associated policies, procedures, processes, and other best practices.
Conduct Information Security awareness and training program to achieve high cybersecurity maturity levels and mitigate risks around lack of awareness.
Ensure that the content of Information Security awareness and training program is reviewed and updated annually based on the results of the previous awareness activities and campaigns, and lessons learnt from security incidents.
Ensure that the Information Security awareness and training program provides sufficient knowledge to all <<CLIENT>> employees, and where applicable, to contractors, third-parties to recognize security incidents and to respond to them using the appropriate reporting channels.
For all new hires, provide induction training that covers information security awareness, to understand its organizational policies and the importance of security with respect to their line of work.
Provided regular training to its technical staff members to implement, configure and maintain security in the information systems using appropriate safeguards.
Promote a culture of security through awareness campaigns.
Utilize security awareness techniques (e.g., displaying digital posters, generating email advisories/ notices from senior organizational officials, phishing simulations etc.) to execute IS campaigns.
Role Specific Training
<<CLIENT>> shall:
Provide Role specific security training for employees/contractors with assigned administrative or technical roles and responsibilities involving access to sensitive information systems.
Administer Role specific security training before granting employees/contractors privileged access to the information systems or before employees/contractors begin performing assigned duties.
Define frequency for Role specific security trainings (e.g. whenever there is a significant change in information system environment or procedures, and whenever an employee/contractor enters a new position that requires additional role-specific training).
Training Records
<<CLIENT>> shall:
Document and monitor individual security training activities, including basic security awareness training and any Role specific security training.
Retain training records for appropriate periods as defined by [<<CLIENT>>-ORG-IMS-POL-013-Records Retention and Protection Policy].
ROLES AND RESPONSIBILITIES
Executive & Senior Management
Commit to provide the required resources and support for implementing a successful Information Security awareness and training program. Required resources shall include financial, technical, and human capital needed for all activities planned for the program.
Where relevant, provide direction to and participate in reviews and oversight of Information Security awareness and training program.
Mandate participation of all <<CLIENT>> employees in IS awareness activities, as outlined within the Information Security awareness and training program.
Information Security
Prepare yearly Security Awareness and Training program and carry out activities as per the plan.
Ensure to record results and report progress to Executive & Senior Management.

Employees, Line Managers and Third Parties (Contractors, Vendors, etc.)
Participate in all awareness and training activities.
Line managers to mandate all employees attend training sessions.
Shall report all violations and security breaches to <<CLIENT>> Information Security Team through the appropriate channels.
Shall acknowledge their understanding and confirm their commitment to follow and comply with all issued policies and procedures.
