data.passage_text:
INTRODUCTION
This Information Risk Management Policy (“Policy”) is a key component of <CLIENTNAME>’s overall Integrated Management System (IMS) and overarches the detailed risk management framework and procedure. Adherence to this Policy will help protect <CLIENTNAME> against known and unknown information security risks, whether internal or external, deliberate or accidental.
<CLIENTNAME> recognizes that effective identification and management of risk is mandatory to the functioning of a robust Integrated Management System (IMS) comprising of Information Security Management System (ISMS), IT Service Management System (ITSMS), Business Continuity Management System (BCMS) and Privacy Information Management System (PIMS).
This Policy will be reviewed at least annually or following any changes to contributing internal or external factors.
PURPOSE
The purpose of this Policy is to ensure that information risks in the organization are identified, assessed, evaluated, monitored, reported and treated according to the business, security, privacy and continuity requirements and objectives of the organization.
Further, the Policy establishes a commitment to comply with applicable sections of the federal laws and regulations, including but not limited to UAE Information Assurance Regulation (UAE IAR) and ISO 27001.
The following are the key objectives of this Policy:
Establish an Information Risk Management Framework: Establishment of an organization-wide Information Security Risk Management Framework.
Address Information Security (IS) risks:  Identification, analysis and evaluation of organizations IS risks to select appropriate IS risk treatment options.
SCOPE
This Policy encompasses all system components that are owned, leased, rented, operated, maintained, and otherwise controlled by <CLIENTNAME> or a third-party on behalf of <CLIENTNAME>, and also applies to any system or system components, both internally and externally, that interact with these systems.
This Policy applies to all employees and contractors who access or manage information and information assets or use the organization's information and communications technology (ICT) systems.
Any exceptions on the applicability of this Policy should be formally requested through the Policy Exception Management procedure.
POLICY STATEMENT
<CLIENTNAME> shall implement an information risk management program that shall require conducting risk assessments, identifying information risks, treating the risks and continuously monitoring risks by evaluating the effectiveness of information security controls to ensure efficient risk handling.
POLICY DETAILS
Information Risk Management Framework
<CLIENTNAME> shall define an Information Risk Management (IRM) framework that provides a process to integrate security, privacy, and business continuity risk management activities into business processes.
The IRM framework shall consider business objectives and constraints placed by applicable laws, directives, executive orders, policies, standards, or regulations.
<CLIENTNAME>’s IRM framework shall include the following activities at a minimum:
Prepare: Establish context by understanding mission, business objectives, and information systems to manage <CLIENTNAME>, its security, privacy, and business continuity risks.
Identify: Provide a structured way to determine the criticality of the information assets at <CLIENTNAME>, and identify its associated risks.
Assess: Analyze, evaluate and categorize risks based on its likelihood of materializing and its impacts to <CLIENTNAME>’s business and information systems.
Select: Select, tailor and document the controls necessary to protect <CLIENTNAME> and its information systems commensurate with risks.
Implement: Establish new or utilize existing processes, procedures, tools and technologies to meet the intent of selected controls.
Review: Determine whether the selected controls are implemented correctly, operate as intended, and produce the desired outcomes to meet <CLIENTNAME>’s information security and data privacy requirements.
Monitor: Maintain ongoing situational awareness about the security and privacy posture of <CLIENTNAME> and its information systems to support risk management decisions.
The IRM framework shall establish and implement a plan for communicating risk information and consulting key stakeholders, including decision-makers within the organization during all stages of the risk management process.
Information Risk Assessment
<CLIENTNAME> shall establish a risk assessment process to be used as the basis for the identification, definition and prioritization of risks.
The risk assessment process shall include at a minimum:
Identification of the vulnerabilities that exist in the information assets.
Identification of threats that may exploit a vulnerability.
Qualitative analysis and evaluation of the impact to the confidentiality, integrity, availability, secrecy and privacy of information assets if a threat exploits vulnerabilities.
Identification and definition of measures and/or controls used to protect the confidentiality, integrity, availability, secrecy and privacy of information assets.
The risk assessment shall be performed/updated when environmental, operational or technical changes arise that impact <CLIENTNAME>’s business processes and information assets. Such changes include but are not limited to:
New threats or risks with respect to the business processes and information assets.
An information security incident.
Outcomes of any internal or external assessments.
Changes to information security requirements or responsibilities. (e.g., new federal or state law or regulation, new or modified security controls implemented, etc.).
Changes to the <CLIENTNAME>’s organizational or technical infrastructure that impact information assets (e.g., addition of a new network, new hardware/software standard implemented, new method of creating, receiving, maintaining or transmitting data, etc.).
Information Risk Categorization
<CLIENTNAME> shall categorize risks based on business objectives, data sensitivity, threat exposure and vulnerabilities pursuant to information assets.
The categorization process shall take into account the probability and impact of risks materializing and affecting business objectives.
Risks shall be expressed qualitatively as per the risk categorizations defined under the Information Risk Management Framework.
Adequate resources to address risks shall be allocated according to the risk categorization.
Information Risk Treatment
<CLIENTNAME> shall select and implement risk treatment measures to reasonably protect the confidentiality, integrity, availability, secrecy and privacy of information assets. One or more of the following methods shall be used to manage risk:
Risk Reduction: Reducing risk by applying security controls.
Risk Avoidance: Avoiding the activity or condition causing the risk.
Risk Transfer: Transferring the risk to another party.
Risk Retention: Accepting the risk based on the organization’s risk acceptance criteria established in the Information Security Risk Management Framework.
<CLIENTNAME> shall document a risk treatment plan to outline the approach to be used to treat the risks. Such risk treatment plans shall include at a minimum:
Description of information risks.
Appropriate management actions.
Responsibilities and priorities for managing information security risks (risk ownership).
Target dates for implementation of the identified controls.
Risk treatment plans shall be monitored and reviewed periodically as deemed necessary to ensure that risk is managed on a continuous basis.
Selection of Controls
<CLIENTNAME> shall select and implement appropriate controls to mitigate identified risks based on relevance, nature, feasibility and cost effectiveness of the controls.
Under the <CLIENTNAME> Integrated Management System (IMS), controls shall be selected from the list of following standards/frameworks/regulations, inclusive of but not limited to:
ISO 27001.
ISO 27701.
ISO 22301.
UAE Information Assurance (IA) Regulation.
Information Risk Monitoring
<CLIENTNAME> shall continuously review and monitor outcomes of risk assessment and treatment. The monitoring processes shall evaluate:
The effectiveness of security controls.
Changes to information assets and environments of operations.
Compliance with federal and state laws and regulations and applicable standards.
ROLES AND RESPONSIBILITIES
7. POLICY ENFORCEMENT AND EXCEPTIONS
All employees and contractors are responsible for understanding and adhering to this Policy. Any violation of this Policy shall be reported to Policy Owner. Violation of this Policy shall be subjected to disciplinary action by the <CLIENTNAME>'s Human Capital Department, taking into consideration the recommendation of IS Team and in accordance with other applicable policies, laws and regulations.
Disciplinary action may range from a verbal warning to discontinuation of access to facility or services, or termination of employment/contract/agreement, and possible legal action. The actual action taken shall depend on factors such as the seriousness of the breach and the user's disciplinary record.
Any exceptions on the applicability of this Policy should be formally requested through the Policy Exception Management procedure.
MONITORING AND REVIEW
This Policy is developed by <CLIENTNAME> Information Security (IS) Team, owned and reviewed by the CISO. It is approved by the CEO.
<CLIENTNAME> IS Team shall initiate a review of this Policy annually or whenever a significant change occurs in the organization's operating environment.
Feedback, suggestions or questions regarding the Policy shall be directed to the <CLIENTNAME> IS Team at infosec@<clientname>.net.

data.heading: The purpose of this  Logging and Monitoring - Full Document
data.passage_text:
INTRODUCTION
A regular monitoring program is key to managing risk in an organization.  Security monitoring occurs on both physical areas as well as logical components in different information system areas.  Information security monitoring confirms that appropriate mechanisms and controls are in place to secure systems and applications, that they are effective, and are not being bypassed in any way.
PURPOSE
The purpose of this  Logging and Monitoring Policy ("Policy") is to define a framework for proactively monitoring events in order to detect potential security incidents quickly and efficiently.
SCOPE
This Policy applies to all employees and third parties who access or manage information and information assets or use the organization's information and communications technology (ICT) systems.
Any exceptions on the applicability of this Policy should be formally requested through the 'Policy Exception Management procedure.’
POLICY STATEMENT
shall develop, implement, and maintain a Logging and Monitoring Policy to secure the confidentiality, integrity, availability, secrecy, and privacy of information and its underlying assets for day-to-day business operations.
POLICY DETAILS
Logging Requirements
Logging requirements shall be determined based on scope, information to be captured in audit logs, frequency for review of audit log data, and applicable laws and regulations.
High level log activities that the company would like to track shall be defined.
Potential log sources shall be identified for log monitoring based upon a risk assessment and their importance to the business.
The critical log sources shall be configured and documented for log generation.
Logs generated for activities that affect the availability and security of the systems, shall be configured for monitoring.
Logging Parameters
Logs shall capture the below details - at minimum:
User IDs.
Date and Time of the event.
Details of the event.
Details of the Asset and its location where possible.
For applications and  following log types shall be monitored - at minimum:
Security Logs.
Administrative and Operator Logs.
Fault Logs.
Backup logs.
Log Storage & Retention
Logs shall be stored in centralized log storage separate from the operational environment in which actual events were generated.
Logs shall be configured in a way that does not affect system performance.
Logging level shall be sufficient to audit individual accountability, reconstruct events, detect intrusions, identify problems and demonstrate regulatory compliance, as may be necessary.
Logs shall be retained for a minimum period of 1 year, or as per the retention period defined by the business owner or in accordance with any legal & regulatory requirements.
Log Reporting
Log reporting shall be performed to summarize significant activity over a particular period of time or to record detailed information related to a particular event or series of events.
The reporting shall capture detail such as top 10 sources/targets, source countries, failed logins etc.
Frequency of log reporting shall be determined based on the criticality of systems and audience.
Log Protection and Access
Adequate security controls shall be taken into account throughout its lifecycle to protect its confidentiality, integrity and availability.
Access to the logs shall be strictly controlled. No user shall be granted permission to modify the logs in an unauthorized manner.
Log repository shall be hardened as per the applicable security baseline documents.
Log Monitoring, Analysis & Review
Monitoring performed by third parties shall involve Non-Disclosure Agreements (NDA) and Service Level Agreements (SLA). The SLA shall include procedures for log reporting and escalation.
Logs generated and stored shall be monitored in real time by a central log monitoring system.
The central log monitoring system shall be capable of centralization, sorting and parsing of the logs collected from different IT Systems to identify, contain and respond quickly to events/incidents in the network
A dedicated monitoring team operating under IT Section shall be assigned to centrally monitor the logs generated by all the IT systems.
Logs shall not be analyzed by the same individual whose activities are being logged and monitored, to ensure segregation of duties.
Corrective and preventive measures on reported incidents shall be taken.
Logs shall be backed up on a regular basis, in accordance with the log storage and retention requirements.
The monitoring team shall, on a periodic basis, submit log analysis reports to the ISSC, Manager – IT Security, application owners and respective system administrators including trends on critical system or security events.
Monitoring systems shall be kept current and regularly tested to ensure that desired events are  and the system performance is efficient, accurate and current.
If a monitoring system fails, an alert mechanism shall be available to alert the concerned monitoring team.
Wherever possible, Intrusion Detection Systems (IDS), Network based Intrusion Detection System (NIDS) and Host-based IDS (HIDS) shall be deployed for effective monitoring of traffic.
Monitoring team shall review and customize IDS alerts regularly to reduce false positives.
Monitoring team shall ensure that the latest attack signatures are updated on the IDS systems. Wherever possible, the IDS systems shall be configured to automatically download new signatures from the vendor site.
The action that needs to be taken for critical alerts shall be identified and documented by the monitoring team in accordance with the Incident Management Procedure.
Time Synchronization
Clock time on all IT systems shall be synchronized with an agreed accurate time source.
ROLES AND RESPONSIBILITIES
POLICY ENFORCEMENT AND EXCEPTIONS
All Employees and 'Third Parties' are responsible for understanding and adhering to this Policy. Any violation of this Policy shall be reported to Policy Owner. In addition, violation of this Policy shall be subjected to disciplinary action by the 's Human Capital Department, considering the recommendation of IS Team and in accordance with other applicable policies, laws, and regulations.
Disciplinary action may range from a verbal warning to discontinuation of access to facility or services, or termination of employment/contract/agreement, and possible legal action. The actual action taken shall depend on factors such as the seriousness of the breach and the user's disciplinary record.
Any exceptions on the applicability of this Policy should be formally requested through the 'Policy Exception Management procedure.'
MONITORING AND REVIEW
This Policy is developed and owned by the  IS Team. It is reviewed by the CISO and approved by the CEO.
IS Team shall initiate a review of this Policy annually or whenever a significant change occurs in the organization's operating environment.
Feedback, suggestions, or questions regarding the Policy shall be directed to the CISO at CISO@.net.

data.heading: The Information Security Incident Management - Full Document
data.passage_text:
INTRODUCTION
The Information Security Incident Management Policy outlines the overall framework for managing security incidents within the organization. It establishes roles and responsibilities for incident detection, response and recovery.
PURPOSE
The purpose of this Information Security Incident Management Policy (“Policy”) is to ensure that information security incidents within the organization are communicated in a manner which allows timely corrective actions to be taken.
Furthermore, the Policy establishes a commitment to comply with applicable sections of the federal and global laws and regulations, including but not limited to UAE Information Assurance Regulation, ISO27001 and National Cyber Risk Management Framework (NCRMF).
The following are the key objectives of this Policy:
Launch an incident response plan: Development, implementation, and maintenance of a plan to guide incident response activities.
Establish a CSIRT: Establishment of a Computer Security Incident Response Team (CSIRT) in charge of the incident management and response plan.
Enable reporting: Reporting information security incidents that results in damage or risk to information security assets and operations through appropriate management channels to stakeholders.
SCOPE
This Policy applies to anybody involved in the security Incident management and/or response including but not limited to employees and critical third parties who access or manage information and information assets or use the organization's information and communications technology (ICT) systems.

Any exceptions on the applicability of this Policy should be formally requested through the ‘Policy Exception Management procedure’.
POLICY STATEMENT
<CLIENT> shall develop, implement, and maintain an incident management program where personnel shall report information security events and weaknesses, collect, and analyze evidence of security incidents, and ensure security incidents are adequately managed.
POLICY DETAILS
Computer Security Incident Response Team (CSIRT)
<CLIENT> shall:
Identify personnel to form the proper CSIRT.
Establish the CSIRT before developing the incident response plan. Ensure that one of the first responsibilities of CSIRT is to create the incident response plan.
Ensure that the CSIRT must include members from the following teams, at a minimum:
Team Leader – is usually senior manager responsible for managing incidents and directing actions to other team members.
Boundary Protection Experts - usually individuals who are experts in logical perimeter security (expertise on Firewalls, IDS/IPS, WAF, Proxy etc.).
Network Administrators.
Physical Security.
Legal
Human Capital for incidents that was originated by an employee.
Corporate Communications for managing the communication of incidents that has been revealed to public.
The CSIRT shall:
Develop incident management and Incident Response plan, process, and procedures.
Response to information security incidents and minimise the impact.
Investigate incidents and try to determine the Root cause.
Prevent future incident by recommending security controls.
Handle incident reporting and communication to all stakeholder involved internally and externally.
Protect collected evidence by ensuring that it is stored in a way that prevents it from being tampered with.
Incident Response Plan
The CSIRT shall:
Develop, implement, and maintain an incident response plan that includes the following, at a minimum:
A roadmap and high-level approach for implementing organization’s incident response capability.
A process to define reportable incidents and metrics for measuring the severity and Impact of incidents.
Define the required resources and management support to effectively maintain and mature an incident response capability.
Documentation that is reviewed and approved by defined personnel or roles.
Distribute copies of the incident response plan to identified stakeholders.
Update incident response plan regularly based on lessons learned.
Communicate incident response plan changes to defined incident response personnel.
Protect the incident response plan from unauthorised disclosure and modification.
Perform investigations based on information distributed by an information sharing community to reduce the risks of similar incidents.
Perform post-incident analysis to identify the source of the incident.
Incident Classification
The CSIRT shall:
Define, classify, and prioritise incidents to help identify the impact and scope of the incident.
Establish an incident classification scheme in line with the incident response plan considering regulatory issuances regarding incident management.
Ensure that the SOC (Security Operations Centre) performs the following, at a minimum when an information security incident is reported:
Assess the information security events using the agreed information security event and incident classification scale.
Decide whether the events should be classified as information security incidents.
Ensure that the assessment of the incident and the associated decision is forwarded to the CSIRT for confirmation and / or reassessment.
Incident Response Training
The CSIRT shall:
Ensure that the incident response training provided by the organisation is linked to assigned roles and responsibilities of respective personnel to include appropriate content and level of detail in such training.
Incident Response Testing
The CSIRT in coordination with other appropriate teams shall:
Develop incident response testing procedures to:
Determine the overall effectiveness of its incident response capabilities.
Identify potential weaknesses or deficiencies.
Ensure that the incident response testing approach simulates pre-defined breach scenarios across the incident response lifecycle including detection, reporting, and recovery to normal operations.
Utilize checklists, table top (discussion-based) exercises, and functional (performance of duties in a simulated environment) exercises for incident response testing.
Participate in sector, national, and international exercises to further test the organization’s incident response capabilities.
Incident Handling
The CSIRT shall:
Respond to information security incidents through a nominated point of contact and other relevant persons of the organization or external parties.
Ensure that the response to incidents include the following activities, at a minimum:
Collecting the evidence as soon as possible after the occurrence.
Conducting information security forensics analysis, as required.
Escalation, as required.
Ensuring all involved response activities are properly logged for later analysis.
Communicating the existence of the information security incident or any relevant details thereof to other internal and external people or organizations with a need-to-know.
Dealing with information security weakness found to cause or contribute to the incident.
Closing and recording the incident after successful resolution.
Information Security Incident Documentation
The CSIRT shall document Information security Incidents with reference to the <CLIENT> Information Security Incident Documentation Procedure.
Learning from Information Security Incidents
The CSIRT shall:
Implement relevant mechanisms to enable the types, volumes, costs (liaising with Finance Team), and impacts of information security incidents to be quantified and monitored.
Utilize the information gained from evaluating information security incidents to identify recurring or high impact incidents and inform risk assessment and risk treatment activities.
Collection of Evidence
<CLIENT> shall:
Develop and follow internal procedures when dealing with evidence for the purposes of disciplinary and legal action.
Include the following processes related to the collection of potential evidence for incidents, at a minimum:
Identification process: the process involving the search for, recognition and documentation of potential evidence.
Collection process: the process of gathering the physical items that can contain potential evidence.
Acquisition process: the process of creating a copy of data within a defined set.
Preservation process: the process to maintain and safeguard the integrity and original condition of the potential evidence.
Establish internal procedures to create the above processes in accordance with different types of media, devices, and state of devices (e.g., powered on or off state).
Ensure that these procedures include the following, at a minimum:
Chain of custody.
Safety of evidence.
Safety of personnel.
Roles and responsibilities of personnel involved.
Competency of personnel.
Documentation.
Briefing.
Situational Awareness
The CSIRT shall:
Ensure priority information related to incidents are shared to relevant stakeholders in a timely manner even if it has not been fully analysed or resolved.
Coordinate and share information in consultation with legal, with external entities, including, but not limited to external service providers, regulatory bodies, entities involved in the supply chain for organization’s information systems.
Provide notice accordingly for all incidents involving Personally Identifiable Information (PII).
ROLES AND RESPONSIBILITIES
POLICY ENFORCEMENT AND EXCEPTIONS
This Policy is to be part of all employees, contractors and third parties’ processes, and all are responsible for understanding and adhering to this Policy. Any violation of this Policy shall be reported to Policy Owner. Violation of this Policy shall be subjected to disciplinary action by the <CLIENT>'s Human Capital Department, taking into consideration the recommendation of the Information Security department and in accordance with other applicable policies, laws and regulations.
Disciplinary action may range from a verbal warning to discontinuation of access to facility or services, or termination of employment/contract/agreement, and possible legal action. The actual action taken shall depend on factors such as the seriousness of the breach and the user's disciplinary record.

Any exceptions on the applicability of this Policy should be formally requested through the Policy Exception Management procedure.
MONITORING AND REVIEW
This Policy is developed and owned by the <CLIENT> Information Security (IS) Team. It is reviewed by the CISO and approved by the CEO.
<CLIENT> IS Team will initiate a review and of this Policy annually or whenever a significant change occurs in the organization’s operating environment.
Feedback, suggestions or questions regarding the Policy shall be directed to the <CLIENT> IS Team at infosec@<CLIENT>.net.

Information security monitoring confirms that appropriate mechanisms and controls are in place to secure systems and applications, that they are effective, and are not being bypassed in any way.
PURPOSE
The purpose of this  Logging and Monitoring Policy ("Policy") is to define a framework for proactively monitoring events in order to detect potential security incidents quickly and efficiently.
SCOPE
This Policy applies to all employees and third parties who access or manage information and information assets or use the organization's information and communications technology (ICT) systems.
Any exceptions on the applicability of this Policy should be formally requested through the 'Policy Exception Management procedure.’
POLICY STATEMENT
shall develop, implement, and maintain a Logging and Monitoring Policy to secure the confidentiality, integrity, availability, secrecy, and privacy of information and its underlying assets for day-to-day business operations.
POLICY DETAILS
Logging Requirements
Logging requirements shall be determined based on scope, information to be captured in audit logs, frequency for review of audit log data, and applicable laws and regulations.
High level log activities that the company would like to track shall be defined.
Potential log sources shall be identified for log monitoring based upon a risk assessment and their importance to the business.
The critical log sources shall be configured and documented for log generation.
Logs generated for activities that affect the availability and security of the systems, shall be configured for monitoring.
Logging Parameters
Logs shall capture the below details - at minimum:
User IDs.
Date and Time of the event.
Details of the event.
Details of the Asset and its location where possible.
For applications and  following log types shall be monitored - at minimum:
Security Logs.
Administrative and Operator Logs.
Fault Logs.
Backup logs.
Log Storage & Retention
Logs shall be stored in centralized log storage separate from the operational environment in which actual events were generated.
Logs shall be configured in a way that does not affect system performance.
Logging level shall be sufficient to audit individual accountability, reconstruct events, detect intrusions, identify problems and demonstrate regulatory compliance, as may be necessary.
Logs shall be retained for a minimum period of 1 year, or as per the retention period defined by the business owner or in accordance with any legal & regulatory requirements.
Log Reporting
Log reporting shall be performed to summarize significant activity over a particular period of time or to record detailed information related to a particular event or series of events.
The reporting shall capture detail such as top 10 sources/targets, source countries, failed logins etc.
Frequency of log reporting shall be determined based on the criticality of systems and audience.
Log Protection and Access
Adequate security controls shall be taken into account throughout its lifecycle to protect its confidentiality, integrity and availability.
Access to the logs shall be strictly controlled. No user shall be granted permission to modify the logs in an unauthorized manner.
Log repository shall be hardened as per the applicable security baseline documents.
Log Monitoring, Analysis & Review
Monitoring performed by third parties shall involve Non-Disclosure Agreements (NDA) and Service Level Agreements (SLA). The SLA shall include procedures for log reporting and escalation.
Logs generated and stored shall be monitored in real time by a central log monitoring system.
The central log monitoring system shall be capable of centralization, sorting and parsing of the logs collected from different IT Systems to identify, contain and respond quickly to events/incidents in the network
A dedicated monitoring team operating under IT Section shall be assigned to centrally monitor the logs generated by all the IT systems.
Logs shall not be analyzed by the same individual whose activities are being logged and monitored, to ensure segregation of duties.
Corrective and preventive measures on reported incidents shall be taken.
Logs shall be backed up on a regular basis, in accordance with the log storage and retention requirements.
The monitoring team shall, on a periodic basis, submit log analysis reports to the ISSC, Manager – IT Security, application owners and respective system administrators including trends on critical system or security events.
Monitoring systems shall be kept current and regularly tested to ensure that desired events are  and the system performance is efficient, accurate and current.
If a monitoring system fails, an alert mechanism shall be available to alert the concerned monitoring team.
Wherever possible, Intrusion Detection Systems (IDS), Network based Intrusion Detection System (NIDS) and Host-based IDS (HIDS) shall be deployed for effective monitoring of traffic.
Monitoring team shall review and customize IDS alerts regularly to reduce false positives.
Monitoring team shall ensure that the latest attack signatures are updated on the IDS systems. Wherever possible, the IDS systems shall be configured to automatically download new signatures from the vendor site.
The action that needs to be taken for critical alerts shall be identified and documented by the monitoring team in accordance with the Incident Management Procedure.
Time Synchronization
Clock time on all IT systems shall be synchronized with an agreed accurate time source.
ROLES AND RESPONSIBILITIES
POLICY ENFORCEMENT AND EXCEPTIONS
All Employees and 'Third Parties' are responsible for understanding and adhering to this Policy. Any violation of this Policy shall be reported to Policy Owner. In addition, violation of this Policy shall be subjected to disciplinary action by the 's Human Capital Department, considering the recommendation of IS Team and in accordance with other applicable policies, laws, and regulations.
Disciplinary action may range from a verbal warning to discontinuation of access to facility or services, or termination of employment/contract/agreement, and possible legal action. The actual action taken shall depend on factors such as the seriousness of the breach and the user's disciplinary record.
Any exceptions on the applicability of this Policy should be formally requested through the 'Policy Exception Management procedure.'
MONITORING AND REVIEW
This Policy is developed and owned by the  IS Team. It is reviewed by the CISO and approved by the CEO.
IS Team shall initiate a review of this Policy annually or whenever a significant change occurs in the organization's operating environment.
Feedback, suggestions, or questions regarding the Policy shall be directed to the CISO at CISO@.net.

data.heading: <CLIENT> employees are expected to understand and comply with this - Full Document
data.passage_text:
INTRODUCTION
<CLIENT> (hereon referred to as <CLIENT>) has embarked on a robust Asset Management initiative to safeguard information assets collected and generated during the normal course of its business operations. As a company that delivers cybersecurity services, <CLIENT> maintains and uses valuable assets. As such, a robust and comprehensive Asset Management program is required to ensure that the assets maintained and operated by <CLIENT> are secured and protected throughout the lifecycle.
<CLIENT> is responsible for identifying assets and how they are used within the company; therefore, it shall set policies, processes, and standard operating procedures for handling assets, implement appropriate technical controls, and educate users about current threats to the assets that they work with and observe best practices for asset protection.
<CLIENT> employees are expected to understand and comply with this Policy and ultimately be responsible for protecting all Information Assets from unauthorized access. Compliance with this Policy is highly important to safeguard the organization's assets and reputation. Any Policy violation may invite appropriate disciplinary action per the corporate policies, applicable laws and regulations in the United Arab Emirates.
PURPOSE
This Asset Management Policy ("Policy") aims to ensure appropriate classification and protection of <CLIENT>'s information and assets.
Further, the Policy establishes a commitment to comply with applicable sections of the federal and global laws and regulations, including but not limited to UAE Information Assurance Regulation, Federal Decree Law No. 45 of 2021 regarding the Protection of Personal Data, ISO 27001, ISO 27701, ISO 20000, ISO 22301 and UAE National Cyber Risk Management Framework (NCRMF).
The following are the key objectives of this Policy:
Establish asset register: Development and maintenance of an asset register.
Handling of information: Correct and consistent handling of information assets in accordance with the organization’s information classification scheme.
Management of media: Correct and consistent management of the asset's lifecycle.
SCOPE
This Policy applies to all employees and critical third parties who access or manage information and information assets or use the organization’s information and communications technology (ICT) systems.
Any exceptions on the applicability of this Policy should be formally requested through the ‘Policy Exception Management procedure’.
POLICY STATEMENT
<CLIENT> shall ensure the security of assets by:
Identifying information assets.
Ensuring that a current and accurate inventory of assets is developed and maintained through an asset register to include responsibilities and classification based on Data Security Policy (<CLIENT>-ORG-IMS-POL-003).
POLICY DETAILS
Asset Inventory
<CLIENT> shall:
Identify assets relevant in the information lifecycle (creation, processing, storage, transmission, deletion, destruction, and protection) and document their importance.
Ensure that all organizational assets are considered for the asset management process using a centralized and efficient approach.
Ensure that documentation is done in dedicated asset inventories as appropriate. The asset inventory at a minimum shall contain:
Type of asset.
Asset ownership.
Location.
Backup information.
Related licenses.
Asset importance/criticality.
Asset Ownership
<CLIENT> shall:
Establish and implement a process to ensure timely assignment of asset ownership.
Assign/reassign ownership when assets are created or transferred.
Assign ownership to individuals having approved management responsibility across the asset lifecycle. Asset owners shall have the following responsibilities at a minimum:
Perform inventory of the applicable assets.
Classify and protect the assets.
Define and periodically review access restrictions and classifications to important assets, considering applicable access control policies in accordance with <CLIENT> Access Control Policy (<CLIENT>-ORG-IMS-POL-017).
Asset Labelling
<CLIENT> shall:
Label information assets in physical and electronic formats according to the established information classification scheme as per the <CLIENT> Data Security Policy (<CLIENT>-ORG-IMS-POL-003).
Develop detailed procedures to give guidance on where and how labels are attached in consideration of how the information is accessed or the assets are handled depending on the types of media.
Ensure that output from systems carry an appropriate classification label aligned to <CLIENT> Data Security Policy (<CLIENT>-ORG-IMS-POL-003).
Ensure labels are easily recognizable and are included in emails, header, and footer of manually created or system-generated documents.
Ensure appropriate awareness is provided to employees and third-party users regarding information labelling procedures.
Asset Handling
<CLIENT> shall:
Develop procedures for handling, processing, storing, and communicating information consistent with its classification level. The following items should be considered, at a minimum:
Access restrictions supporting the protection requirements for each level of classification.
Protection of temporary or permanent copies of information to a level consistent with the protection of the original information.
Storage of IT assets in accordance with manufacturers' specifications.
Safeguard information in accordance with the established procedures.
Ensure appropriate awareness is provided to employees and third-party users regarding the information handling procedures.
Removable Media Management
<CLIENT> shall:
Ensure that all information is stored only in organization authorized media.
Ensure that contents of any re-usable media that are to be removed from the organization is made unrecoverable when no longer required.
Ensure that appropriate authorization is obtained for media removed from the organization and a record of such removals is kept in order to maintain an audit trail.
Store all media in a safe and secure environment in accordance with manufacturers' specifications.
Apply cryptographic techniques to protect data on removable media.
Ensure that all removable media is scanned for malware before utilization.
Ensure transfer of data to fresh media before it gets unreadable.
Securely store/backup multiple copies of valuable data on separate media to further reduce the risk of incidental and/or accidental data damage or loss.
Restrict and monitor the usage of removable media to limit the opportunity for data loss.
Ensure that any organization-controlled media with data is not sent to the manufacturer for any repair purposes to avoid leakage of residual confidential information.
Disposal of Media
<CLIENT> shall:
Establish procedures for secure disposal of media containing organizational information based on the sensitivity of that information.
Ensure that the procedures are defined to identify media devices that might require secure disposal.
Ensure that media, both paper and digital, are destroyed when no longer serving the organization.
Ensure that records of disposed media (containing or used to contain confidential information) are kept when no longer needed.
Physical Media Transfer
<CLIENT> shall:
Utilize reliable transport or couriers for transportation of physical media between sites.
Develop a list of authorized couriers that are approved by the management.
Define labelling requirements for physical media carrying sensitive information.
Ensure that logs of the transfer are kept identifying the content of the media, protection applied, time(s) of transfer to the transit custodian(s), and the receipt at destination.
Consider measures to be taken in the event of loss of physical media in transit carrying sensitive information.
Acceptable Use of Assets
<CLIENT> shall:
Ensure that usage of all organization’s information, information assets and information and communications technology (ICT) systems are approved by the information security department prior to deployment.
Establish, document, and circulate the rules for the acceptable use of assets in line with the <CLIENT> Acceptable Use Policy (<CLIENT>-ORG-IMS-POL-002) and disciplinary procedures.
Consider the information provided by other members of an information sharing community as an asset and protect and disseminate in accordance with agreed upon rules.
Bring Your Own Device (BYOD) Arrangements
<CLIENT> shall:
Provide means to securely access organizational information and information assets via ‘Bring Your Own Device’ (BYOD).
Establish and implement information security requirements to secure organizational information and information assets via ‘Bring Your Own Device’ (BYOD) in line with <CLIENT> Bring Your Own Device (BYOD) Policy (<CLIENT>-ORG-IMS-POL-020).
ROLES AND RESPONSIBILITIES
POLICY ENFORCEMENT AND EXCEPTIONS
This Policy is to be part of all Employees' and Third Parties' processes, and all are responsible for understanding and adhering to this Policy. Any violation of this Policy shall be reported to Policy Owner. Violation of this Policy shall be subjected to disciplinary action by the <CLIENT>'s Human Capital Department, taking into consideration the recommendation of the Information Security department and in accordance with other applicable policies, laws and regulations.
Disciplinary action may range from a verbal warning to discontinuation of access to facility or services, or termination of employment/contract/agreement, and possible legal action. The actual action taken shall depend on factors such as the seriousness of the breach and the user's disciplinary record.
Any exceptions on the applicability of this Policy should be formally requested through the Policy Exception Management procedure.
MONITORING AND REVIEW
This Policy is developed and owned by the <CLIENT> Information Security (IS) Team. It is reviewed by the CISO and approved by the CEO.
<CLIENT> IS Team will initiate a review and of this Policy annually or whenever a significant change occurs in the organization’s operating environment.
Feedback, suggestions or questions regarding the Policy shall be directed to the <CLIENT> IS Team at infosec@<CLIENT>.com.

data.passage_id: clientname-IS-POL-00-Physical and Environmental Security Policy v1.2_1
data.heading: The purpose of this Physical and Environmental Security - Full Document
data.passage_text:
INTRODUCTION
Physical and environmental security refers to measures taken to protect an organization’s information systems, facilities, and related infrastructure from threats against the physical environment. Physical and environmental safeguards are often overlooked, but are very important in protecting information. Facilities that house information and Information Technology (IT) resources shall be afforded appropriate protection to avoid damage or unauthorized access to information and systems. In addition, equipment that store information shall be physically protected. <CLIENTNAME> is committed to providing effective physical and environmental protection mechanisms in order to safeguard information assets from unauthorized disclosure and damage as well as other environmental calamities.
PURPOSE
The purpose of this Physical and Environmental Security Policy (“Policy”) is to ensure that physical access to organization’s premises is controlled, monitored and security measures are implemented to safeguard its users, assets and premises against physical security risks and environmental hazards.
Further, the Policy establishes a commitment to comply with applicable sections of the federal laws and regulations including but not limited to ISO 27001 and UAE Information Assurance (IA) Regulation.
The following are the key objectives of this Policy:
Definition of security perimeters: Definition and use of security perimeters to protect areas that contain either sensitive or critical information, or information processing facilities.
Application of physical and environmental protection: Design and implementation of physical and environmental protection for secure areas and guidelines for working in secure areas.
SCOPE
This Policy applies to all physical areas within <CLIENTNAME>, employees and third parties who access <CLIENTNAME> facilities, information assets and information and communications technology (ICT) systems housed within <CLIENTNAME> facilities.
Any exceptions on the applicability of this Policy should be formally requested through the ‘Policy Exception Management procedure’.
POLICY STATEMENT
<CLIENTNAME> shall implement physical and environmental security measures to ensure critical or sensitive information systems are physically protected from unauthorized access, damage, and interference. Further <CLIENTNAME> shall implement controls to protect people and equipment from physical and environmental threats.
POLICY DETAILS
Establish a Physical Security Perimeter
<CLIENTNAME> shall:
Identify and classify security areas based on the assets and information they process or contain.
Define security perimeters, establish appropriate countermeasures such as mentioned below, and test as appropriately deemed for every classified security level to ensure the right level of protection is applied:
Supervision of reception area.
Physical Barriers to company premises.
Restrict access to authorized personnel and install intruder detection systems for external doors, accessible windows.
Fire doors are alarmed, monitored, and tested in accordance with suitable regional, national, and international standards.
Suppression systems are installed, monitored, and tested.
Implement Physical Security Controls
<CLIENTNAME> shall:
Record the date, time of entry and departure of employees/contractors/visitors and others who seek access to <CLIENTNAME> facilities.
Perform physical security checks for all personnel entering the organizational premises.
Restrict the entry and usage of devices within organizational premises according to security requirements.
Supervise visitors at all times during their visit to secure areas.
Verify the identity of visitors requesting access, by an appropriate means.
Ensure that all employees, contractors, and external parties wear some form of visible identification within the organization’s premises.
Grant access only for specific, authorized purposes to visitors and provide clear instructions on the security requirements of the area and on emergency procedures.
Document and implement a process to authenticate all persons accessing secure areas. Ensure that access is provided to secure areas for only authorized personnel by implementing access controls.
Ensure that access to secure areas is logged. Ensure that access logs are maintained and monitored.
Ensure access rights are regularly reviewed, updated, and revoked when necessary.
Ensure that sensitive personnel data (e.g., biometric data used for access controls) is protected as per applicable data privacy laws and regulations wherever required.
<CLIENTNAME> personnel shall:
Always escort Contractors/third parties unless the area is explicitly designated for them with no escort requirement.
Notify security personnel immediately if they encounter unescorted visitors or anyone not wearing visible identification.
Protecting Against External and Environmental Threats
<CLIENTNAME> shall:
Provide adequate training to personnel responsible for responding to environmental and safety hazards.
Ensure and monitor power, temperature and relative humidity inside data centers are in accordance with relevant regulations.
Ensure data centers have 24/7 air conditioning and humidity control.
Conduct planned maintenance for all supporting utilities and necessary records.
Activate fire suppression system only in affected areas to limit the damage on other assets.
Site equipment appropriately to prevent unauthorized access into work areas.
Monitor your work environmental conditions and alert in case of a potential threat.
Working in Secure Areas
<CLIENTNAME> shall:
Establish guidelines for working in secure areas.
Make sure all personnel accessing secure areas are aware of and accept organization’s rules and guidelines.
Protect Delivery and Loading Areas
<CLIENTNAME> shall:
Establish access procedures to loading and unloading areas to restrict access to only authorized personnel.
Design the delivery and loading area where supplies can be loaded and unloaded without delivery personnel gaining access to other parts of the building.
Establish and implement procedures for the storage of hazardous or combustible materials to reduce their risks on secure areas.
Physically segregate loading and unloading activities.
<CLIENTNAME> personnel shall:
Perform appropriate due diligence and implement countermeasures to securely manage incoming and outgoing materials and equipment.
Ensure Effective Supporting Utilities
<CLIENTNAME> shall:
Ensure that each facility’s supporting utilities (e.g., electricity, telecommunications, water supply, natural gas, sewage, heating ventilation and air conditioning) are in line with local legal requirements and manufacturer’s specifications as well as tested for malfunctions.
Conduct preventive maintenance for all supporting utilities and necessary records maintained for the same.
Cabling Security
<CLIENTNAME> shall:
Ensure that power and telecommunications lines into information systems are underground wherever possible.
Protect power and telecommunication lines adequately from interception of data or damage.
Physically separate power cables from communications cables to minimize interference.
Shield telecommunication cables to avoid information leakage due to electro-magnetic signals emanations.
Consider the following controls for sensitive or critical systems:
Installation of armored conduit and locked rooms or boxes at inspection and termination points.
Utilization of electromagnetic shielding to protect the cables.
Initiation of technical sweeps and physical inspections for unauthorized devices being attached to the cables.
Controlled access to patch panels and cable rooms.
Security of Equipment Off-Premises
<CLIENTNAME> shall:
Establish an authorization procedure for taking information assets off-site.
Ensure manufacturers' instructions for protecting equipment is adhered to at all times.
Maintain the list of information assets off-site with the authorized employees and external party users.
The personnel shall:
Ensure that equipment and media taken off the premises are not left unattended in public places.
Secure Disposal or Reuse of Equipment
<CLIENTNAME> shall:
Ensure storage media containing confidential information is physically destroyed or the information is destroyed, deleted, or overwritten using techniques to make the original information non-retrievable rather than using the standard delete or format function.
ROLES AND RESPONSIBILITIES
POLICY ENFORCEMENT AND EXCEPTIONS
This Policy is to be part of all Employees' and Third Parties' processes, and all are responsible for understanding and adhering to this Policy. Any violation of this Policy shall be reported to Policy Owner. Violation of this Policy shall be subjected to disciplinary action by the <CLIENTNAME>'s Human Capital Department, taking into consideration the recommendation of the Information Security department and in accordance with other applicable policies, laws and regulations.
Disciplinary action may range from a verbal warning to discontinuation of access to facility or services, or termination of employment/contract/agreement, and possible legal action. The actual action taken shall depend on factors such as the seriousness of the breach and the user's disciplinary record.

Any exceptions on the applicability of this Policy should be formally requested through the ‘Policy Exception Management procedure’.
MONITORING AND REVIEW
This Policy is developed and owned by the <CLIENTNAME> Information Security (IS) Team. It is reviewed by the CISO and approved by the CEO.
<CLIENTNAME> IS Team will initiate a review and of this Policy annually or whenever a significant change occurs in the organization’s operating environment.
Feedback, suggestions or questions regarding the Policy shall be directed to the <CLIENTNAME> IS Team at infosec@<clientname>.net.

data.heading: The Information Security Incident Management - Full Document
data.passage_text:
INTRODUCTION
The Information Security Incident Management Policy outlines the overall framework for managing security incidents within the organization. It establishes roles and responsibilities for incident detection, response and recovery.
PURPOSE
The purpose of this Information Security Incident Management Policy (“Policy”) is to ensure that information security incidents within the organization are communicated in a manner which allows timely corrective actions to be taken.
Furthermore, the Policy establishes a commitment to comply with applicable sections of the federal and global laws and regulations, including but not limited to UAE Information Assurance Regulation, ISO27001 and National Cyber Risk Management Framework (NCRMF).
The following are the key objectives of this Policy:
Launch an incident response plan: Development, implementation, and maintenance of a plan to guide incident response activities.
Establish a CSIRT: Establishment of a Computer Security Incident Response Team (CSIRT) in charge of the incident management and response plan.
Enable reporting: Reporting information security incidents that results in damage or risk to information security assets and operations through appropriate management channels to stakeholders.
SCOPE
This Policy applies to anybody involved in the security Incident management and/or response including but not limited to employees and critical third parties who access or manage information and information assets or use the organization's information and communications technology (ICT) systems.

Any exceptions on the applicability of this Policy should be formally requested through the ‘Policy Exception Management procedure’.
POLICY STATEMENT
<CLIENT> shall develop, implement, and maintain an incident management program where personnel shall report information security events and weaknesses, collect, and analyze evidence of security incidents, and ensure security incidents are adequately managed.
POLICY DETAILS
Computer Security Incident Response Team (CSIRT)
<CLIENT> shall:
Identify personnel to form the proper CSIRT.
Establish the CSIRT before developing the incident response plan. Ensure that one of the first responsibilities of CSIRT is to create the incident response plan.
Ensure that the CSIRT must include members from the following teams, at a minimum:
Team Leader – is usually senior manager responsible for managing incidents and directing actions to other team members.
Boundary Protection Experts - usually individuals who are experts in logical perimeter security (expertise on Firewalls, IDS/IPS, WAF, Proxy etc.).
Network Administrators.
Physical Security.
Legal
Human Capital for incidents that was originated by an employee.
Corporate Communications for managing the communication of incidents that has been revealed to public.
The CSIRT shall:
Develop incident management and Incident Response plan, process, and procedures.
Response to information security incidents and minimise the impact.
Investigate incidents and try to determine the Root cause.
Prevent future incident by recommending security controls.
Handle incident reporting and communication to all stakeholder involved internally and externally.
Protect collected evidence by ensuring that it is stored in a way that prevents it from being tampered with.
Incident Response Plan
The CSIRT shall:
Develop, implement, and maintain an incident response plan that includes the following, at a minimum:
A roadmap and high-level approach for implementing organization’s incident response capability.
A process to define reportable incidents and metrics for measuring the severity and Impact of incidents.
Define the required resources and management support to effectively maintain and mature an incident response capability.
Documentation that is reviewed and approved by defined personnel or roles.
Distribute copies of the incident response plan to identified stakeholders.
Update incident response plan regularly based on lessons learned.
Communicate incident response plan changes to defined incident response personnel.
Protect the incident response plan from unauthorised disclosure and modification.
Perform investigations based on information distributed by an information sharing community to reduce the risks of similar incidents.
Perform post-incident analysis to identify the source of the incident.
Incident Classification
The CSIRT shall:
Define, classify, and prioritise incidents to help identify the impact and scope of the incident.
Establish an incident classification scheme in line with the incident response plan considering regulatory issuances regarding incident management.
Ensure that the SOC (Security Operations Centre) performs the following, at a minimum when an information security incident is reported:
Assess the information security events using the agreed information security event and incident classification scale.
Decide whether the events should be classified as information security incidents.
Ensure that the assessment of the incident and the associated decision is forwarded to the CSIRT for confirmation and / or reassessment.
Incident Response Training
The CSIRT shall:
Ensure that the incident response training provided by the organisation is linked to assigned roles and responsibilities of respective personnel to include appropriate content and level of detail in such training.
Incident Response Testing
The CSIRT in coordination with other appropriate teams shall:
Develop incident response testing procedures to:
Determine the overall effectiveness of its incident response capabilities.
Identify potential weaknesses or deficiencies.
Ensure that the incident response testing approach simulates pre-defined breach scenarios across the incident response lifecycle including detection, reporting, and recovery to normal operations.
Utilize checklists, table top (discussion-based) exercises, and functional (performance of duties in a simulated environment) exercises for incident response testing.
Participate in sector, national, and international exercises to further test the organization’s incident response capabilities.
Incident Handling
The CSIRT shall:
Respond to information security incidents through a nominated point of contact and other relevant persons of the organization or external parties.
Ensure that the response to incidents include the following activities, at a minimum:
Collecting the evidence as soon as possible after the occurrence.
Conducting information security forensics analysis, as required.
Escalation, as required.
Ensuring all involved response activities are properly logged for later analysis.
Communicating the existence of the information security incident or any relevant details thereof to other internal and external people or organizations with a need-to-know.
Dealing with information security weakness found to cause or contribute to the incident.
Closing and recording the incident after successful resolution.
Information Security Incident Documentation
The CSIRT shall document Information security Incidents with reference to the <CLIENT> Information Security Incident Documentation Procedure.
Learning from Information Security Incidents
The CSIRT shall:
Implement relevant mechanisms to enable the types, volumes, costs (liaising with Finance Team), and impacts of information security incidents to be quantified and monitored.
Utilize the information gained from evaluating information security incidents to identify recurring or high impact incidents and inform risk assessment and risk treatment activities.
Collection of Evidence
<CLIENT> shall:
Develop and follow internal procedures when dealing with evidence for the purposes of disciplinary and legal action.
Include the following processes related to the collection of potential evidence for incidents, at a minimum:
Identification process: the process involving the search for, recognition and documentation of potential evidence.
Collection process: the process of gathering the physical items that can contain potential evidence.
Acquisition process: the process of creating a copy of data within a defined set.
Preservation process: the process to maintain and safeguard the integrity and original condition of the potential evidence.
Establish internal procedures to create the above processes in accordance with different types of media, devices, and state of devices (e.g., powered on or off state).
Ensure that these procedures include the following, at a minimum:
Chain of custody.
Safety of evidence.
Safety of personnel.
Roles and responsibilities of personnel involved.
Competency of personnel.
Documentation.
Briefing.
Situational Awareness
The CSIRT shall:
Ensure priority information related to incidents are shared to relevant stakeholders in a timely manner even if it has not been fully analysed or resolved.
Coordinate and share information in consultation with legal, with external entities, including, but not limited to external service providers, regulatory bodies, entities involved in the supply chain for organization’s information systems.
Provide notice accordingly for all incidents involving Personally Identifiable Information (PII).
ROLES AND RESPONSIBILITIES
POLICY ENFORCEMENT AND EXCEPTIONS
This Policy is to be part of all employees, contractors and third parties’ processes, and all are responsible for understanding and adhering to this Policy. Any violation of this Policy shall be reported to Policy Owner. Violation of this Policy shall be subjected to disciplinary action by the <CLIENT>'s Human Capital Department, taking into consideration the recommendation of the Information Security department and in accordance with other applicable policies, laws and regulations.
Disciplinary action may range from a verbal warning to discontinuation of access to facility or services, or termination of employment/contract/agreement, and possible legal action. The actual action taken shall depend on factors such as the seriousness of the breach and the user's disciplinary record.

Any exceptions on the applicability of this Policy should be formally requested through the Policy Exception Management procedure.
MONITORING AND REVIEW
This Policy is developed and owned by the <CLIENT> Information Security (IS) Team. It is reviewed by the CISO and approved by the CEO.
<CLIENT> IS Team will initiate a review and of this Policy annually or whenever a significant change occurs in the organization’s operating environment.
Feedback, suggestions or questions regarding the Policy shall be directed to the <CLIENT> IS Team at infosec@<CLIENT>.net.

data.heading: The Compliance - Implement appropriate processes, controls, responsibilities and system requirements to ensure compliance with intellectual property rights, including but not limited to:
data.passage_text:
5.2.1 Implement appropriate processes, controls, responsibilities and system requirements to ensure compliance with intellectual property rights, including but not limited to:
5.2.1.a Maintaining appropriate asset registers and identify all assets with requirements to protect intellectual property rights.
5.2.1.b Acquiring software only through known and reputable sources to ensure that no intellectual property rights are violated.
5.2.1.c Performing verification to ensure that only authorized software and licensed products are installed.
5.2.1.d Enhancing and maintaining awareness to protect intellectual property rights and intent of disciplinary action against employees who breach them.
5.2.1.e Ensuring that proprietary software products are under a license agreement that specifies license terms and conditions.
5.2.1.f Dealing software products created internally, or licensed/provided by any developer to <<CLIENT>> as <<CLIENT>> proprietary material.
5.2.1.g Operational processes for maintaining proof and evidence of ownership of licenses, master disks, manuals, etc.

5.3.1    Define and categorize records by record type (e.g. financial, database, transaction logs, audit logs, operational procedures) and include details of retention periods and type of storage media.
Store related cryptographic key material to permit decryption of records for the period the records are retained or archived.
Consider the potential deterioration of media used for storing digital records when protecting them.
Develop procedures for ensuring access and availability of data to safeguard against loss due to technology changes in case of long-term storage.
Collect, process, transmit, store, and/ or delete personal data fairly and lawfully.
Collect and process personal data only for the intended business purpose. Collected and processed personal data shall be acknowledged by the individual concerned (data subject).
Keep personal data up to date and factual. Inaccurate or incomplete personal data shall be deleted, corrected, supplemented, or updated.
Delete personal data that is no longer required for the intended business purpose.
Implement appropriate technical and organizational measures to protect personal information.
Develop processes and procedures to ensure that personal data is protected throughout its lifecycle.
Compliance with Security Policies and Standards
<<CLIENT>> shall:
Conduct compliance assessments periodically with respect to applicable standards, laws and regulations.
Report compliance results to appropriate personnel and senior management.
Take necessary corrective actions identified.
Record, monitor and review corrective actions to ensure compliance is maintained.
ROLES AND RESPONSIBILITIES
Executives & Senior Management
Make available and support the required resources to drive Information Security Compliance.
Where relevant, provide direction to and participate in reviews and oversight of Information Security Compliance.
Review and approve this Policy and all related procedures prepared by the <<CLIENT>> Information Security team.
Review and approve the process developed by Information Security to identify ongoing compliance requirements applicable to the organization.
Initiate independent reviews (Internal Audit and/or External Audit) of information security at planned intervals or when a significant change occurs.
IS (Information Security) Team
Determine specific system requirements resulting from the identified compliance requirements.
Define specific controls to ensure:
All intellectual property right protection requirements are met.
All privacy and protection of personally identifiable information requirements are met.
All record protection requirements are met for records with specific compliance requirements.

Ensure that external member’s provisions are accounted for in the organization's information systems audit plan and tools.

Report any issues detected during technical compliance checking to the appropriate authority for remediation.
IT (Information Technology) Team
Apply specific controls to ensure all compliance requirements are met.

Take corrective action when an independent review identifies that the organization's approach and implementation to managing information security is inadequate.
Human Capital
Assist IS team to determine specific system requirements resulting from the identified (privacy) requirements.
Ensure the individuals carrying out independent reviews have the appropriate skills and experience to conduct the review.
Employees and Third Parties (Contractors, Vendors, etc.)
Employees and third parties (including contractors, vendors, etc.) who access organizational premises, information and information assets or use information and communications technology (ICT) systems of the organization are responsible for reporting any compliance violation with respect to this Policy and all its applicable procedures.
Legal
Define a process for identifying data with specific compliance requirements regarding privacy and protection of personally identifiable information.
Identify all relevant and applicable laws and regulations to <<CLIENT>> and communicate to the respective parties.
Define and liaise with IS Team on standard legal clauses and terms for the contract/agreement to be signed with external parties, employment contracts, confidentiality and Non-Disclosure Agreements (NDAs).
Review third-party vendor, clients and partner contracts and involve IS Team for review of clauses concerning Information Security responsibilities.
POLICY ENFORCEMENT AND EXCEPTIONS
All employees, contractors and third parties are responsible for understanding and adhering to this Policy. Any violation of this Policy shall be reported to Policy Owner. Violation of this Policy shall be subjected to disciplinary action by the <<CLIENT>>'s Human Capital Department, taking into consideration the recommendation of IS Team and in accordance with other applicable policies, laws and regulations.
Disciplinary action may range from a verbal warning to discontinuation of access to facility or services, or termination of employment/contract/agreement, and possible legal action. The actual action taken shall depend on factors such as the seriousness of the breach and the user's disciplinary record.
Any exceptions on the applicability of this Policy should be formally requested through the Policy Exception Management procedure.
MONITORING AND REVIEW
This Policy is developed by <<CLIENT>> Information Security (IS) Team, owned and reviewed by the CISO. It is approved by the CEO.
