data.heading: This Security Operations - Full Document
data.passage_text:
INTRODUCTION
Security operations  to the combination of information security (IS) and IT operations to improve collaboration and reduce risks.
This Security Operations Policy (“Policy”) defines how Security Operations will be planned, established, implemented, operated, monitored, reviewed, maintained, and improved within . This Policy and its supporting policies set the premise for the organization's objective of safeguarding its information and underlying assets against internal and external threats continually strengthening its security posture.
PURPOSE
The purpose of this Policy is to protect data/Information and information processing facilities through secure and effective operations at .
SCOPE
This Policy applies to all employees, contractors and third parties who access or manage information and information assets or use the organization's information and communications technology (ICT) systems.
Any exceptions on the applicability of this Policy should be formally requested through the 'Policy Exception Management procedure.'
POLICY STATEMENT
shall develop, implement, and maintain a Security Operations Policy ("Policy")  protection of assets through secure operational processes thereby contributing to an overall safe and secure organization.
POLICY DETAILS
Documenting the Operating Procedures
shall ensure that the:
IT Team ensures that Operating procedures and work instructions for all information systems and standards are:
Documented and approved.
Reviewed and updated periodically when there are:
Alterations to the designs.
Changes to Equipment/Systems Technology and Configurations.
Changes to business services.
Various activities covered under IT operations are inclusive of but are not limited to:
Change Management.
Release Management.
Software Development.
Problem Management.
Incident Management.
Operating procedures specify the instructions for the execution of each task, as appropriate, including backup and restoration of data/information, error handling, processing and handling of information, system restart and recovery procedures for use in the event of system failure.
IT Team ensures that procedures are kept up-to-date are available to the users when needed.
Activities shall be carried out in accordance with operating procedures.
Controls ver System Documentation
shall ensure that:
System documentation is kept under safe custody.
Access is restricted to only those who have a business need and are authorized.
Version control is implemented to ensure that the current version is consistently available.
Copying of any system documentation shall be controlled and shall be allowed only after authorization.
The respective custodians of documents shall hold the original set of the system documentation.
Separation of Development, Test and Operational Environments
shall ensure that:
Production environment shall be logically separated from test and development environments.
Access to each environment shall be controlled using firewalls.
Transfer of software between the development, test and production environments shall be subject to specific procedures such as the  Information Systems Acquisition, Development and Maintenance Procedure and will be documented.
Development staff shall not have access to the production environment.
Production data files shall not be used for application development and testing. When such use is necessitated, copies of the production data files will be made redacted/obfuscated, sanitized and taken offline for that purpose. Those copies shall be handled carefully and later deleted to prevent unauthorized exposure of production data.
Purchasing, Installation, Allocation and Management of New IT Systems
shall ensure that:
All purchases of software, IT equipment and peripherals shall follow a standard evaluation process, documented and done using approved vendors only.
All software and hardware installations shall be tested prior to being moved to live production environment by both IT and business users. Any changes to software or hardware configuration shall be appropriately authorized.
Capacity requirements shall be monitored to avoid failures due to inadequate capacity.
The IT team establishes and implements a formal process for capacity management which shall include following periodic activities:
Review and analysis of the resource utilization reports and trends.
Review and analysis of the business plan covering ongoing and upcoming IT projects.
Develop capacity plans to meet the current and projected demands for capacity, power and reliability of systems and networks.
Conduct appropriate load and stress tests and define acceptable capacity levels for new or upgraded enterprise applications.
Allocation of information systems (Laptops, PCs, tablet PCs etc.) shall be as per an established process through allocation form.
Any changes to existing systems (such as Laptops, PCs, Servers, Databases etc.) shall be as per the  Change Management Policy and procedure.
All critical servers and databases shall be tested (Vulnerability Assessment and Penetration Testing), periodically for inherent vulnerabilities or those which are developed over the time.
System Acceptance
shall ensure that:
System acceptance tests are done in an appropriate test environment.
Before moving any new system into production, it shall be tested as per the System Acceptance Plan.
An appropriate System Acceptance Plan shall be established to cover:
’s system performance requirements.
’s Security requirements.
’s business continuity requirements.
Error recovery and restart procedures.
Effects of the new system on other existing systems.
User training requirements and ease of use of new systems, so as to ensure desired level of user performance.
Backup Plan, Roll Back Plan and DR (Disaster Recovery) Plan.
The system is sanitized after successful testing, the system which includes removal of test users, dummy transactions and test data.
Segregation of Duties
shall:
Adopt the principle of segregation of duties to the maximum extent possible.
Include compensating controls such as monitoring of activities, maintenance and review of audit trails and management supervision where segregation of duties is not possible or practical.
ROLES AND RESPONSIBILITIES
POLICY ENFORCEMENT AND EXCEPTIONS
All employees, contractors and third parties are responsible for understanding and adhering to this Policy. Any violation of this Policy shall be reported to Policy Owner. In addition, violation of this Policy shall be subjected to disciplinary action by the 's Human Capital Department, considering the recommendation of IS Team and in accordance with other applicable policies, laws, and regulations.
Disciplinary action may range from a verbal warning to discontinuation of access to facility or services, or termination of employment/contract/agreement, and possible legal action. The actual action taken shall depend on factors such as the seriousness of the breach and the user's disciplinary record.
Any exceptions on the applicability of this Policy should be formally requested through the 'Policy Exception Management procedure.'
MONITORING AND REVIEW
This Policy is developed and owned by the  Information Security (IS) Team. It is reviewed by the CISO and approved by the CEO.
IS Team shall initiate a review of this Policy annually or whenever a significant change occurs in the organization's operating environment.
Feedback, suggestions, or questions regarding the Policy shall be directed to the CISO at CISO@.net.

data.heading: This Information Risk Management - Full Document
data.passage_text:
INTRODUCTION
This Information Risk Management Policy (“Policy”) is a key component of <CLIENTNAME>’s overall Integrated Management System (IMS) and overarches the detailed risk management framework and procedure. Adherence to this Policy will help protect <CLIENTNAME> against known and unknown information security risks, whether internal or external, deliberate or accidental.
<CLIENTNAME> recognizes that effective identification and management of risk is mandatory to the functioning of a robust Integrated Management System (IMS) comprising of Information Security Management System (ISMS), IT Service Management System (ITSMS), Business Continuity Management System (BCMS) and Privacy Information Management System (PIMS).
This Policy will be reviewed at least annually or following any changes to contributing internal or external factors.
PURPOSE
The purpose of this Policy is to ensure that information risks in the organization are identified, assessed, evaluated, monitored, reported and treated according to the business, security, privacy and continuity requirements and objectives of the organization.
Further, the Policy establishes a commitment to comply with applicable sections of the federal laws and regulations, including but not limited to UAE Information Assurance Regulation (UAE IAR) and ISO 27001.
The following are the key objectives of this Policy:
Establish an Information Risk Management Framework: Establishment of an organization-wide Information Security Risk Management Framework.
Address Information Security (IS) risks:  Identification, analysis and evaluation of organizations IS risks to select appropriate IS risk treatment options.
SCOPE
This Policy encompasses all system components that are owned, leased, rented, operated, maintained, and otherwise controlled by <CLIENTNAME> or a third-party on behalf of <CLIENTNAME>, and also applies to any system or system components, both internally and externally, that interact with these systems.
This Policy applies to all employees and contractors who access or manage information and information assets or use the organization's information and communications technology (ICT) systems.
Any exceptions on the applicability of this Policy should be formally requested through the Policy Exception Management procedure.
POLICY STATEMENT
<CLIENTNAME> shall implement an information risk management program that shall require conducting risk assessments, identifying information risks, treating the risks and continuously monitoring risks by evaluating the effectiveness of information security controls to ensure efficient risk handling.
POLICY DETAILS
Information Risk Management Framework
<CLIENTNAME> shall define an Information Risk Management (IRM) framework that provides a process to integrate security, privacy, and business continuity risk management activities into business processes.
The IRM framework shall consider business objectives and constraints placed by applicable laws, directives, executive orders, policies, standards, or regulations.
<CLIENTNAME>’s IRM framework shall include the following activities at a minimum:
Prepare: Establish context by understanding mission, business objectives, and information systems to manage <CLIENTNAME>, its security, privacy, and business continuity risks.
Identify: Provide a structured way to determine the criticality of the information assets at <CLIENTNAME>, and identify its associated risks.
Assess: Analyze, evaluate and categorize risks based on its likelihood of materializing and its impacts to <CLIENTNAME>’s business and information systems.
Select: Select, tailor and document the controls necessary to protect <CLIENTNAME> and its information systems commensurate with risks.
Implement: Establish new or utilize existing processes, procedures, tools and technologies to meet the intent of selected controls.
Review: Determine whether the selected controls are implemented correctly, operate as intended, and produce the desired outcomes to meet <CLIENTNAME>’s information security and data privacy requirements.
Monitor: Maintain ongoing situational awareness about the security and privacy posture of <CLIENTNAME> and its information systems to support risk management decisions.
The IRM framework shall establish and implement a plan for communicating risk information and consulting key stakeholders, including decision-makers within the organization during all stages of the risk management process.
Information Risk Assessment
<CLIENTNAME> shall establish a risk assessment process to be used as the basis for the identification, definition and prioritization of risks.
The risk assessment process shall include at a minimum:
Identification of the vulnerabilities that exist in the information assets.
Identification of threats that may exploit a vulnerability.
Qualitative analysis and evaluation of the impact to the confidentiality, integrity, availability, secrecy and privacy of information assets if a threat exploits vulnerabilities.
Identification and definition of measures and/or controls used to protect the confidentiality, integrity, availability, secrecy and privacy of information assets.
The risk assessment shall be performed/updated when environmental, operational or technical changes arise that impact <CLIENTNAME>’s business processes and information assets. Such changes include but are not limited to:
New threats or risks with respect to the business processes and information assets.
An information security incident.
Outcomes of any internal or external assessments.
Changes to information security requirements or responsibilities. (e.g., new federal or state law or regulation, new or modified security controls implemented, etc.).
Changes to the <CLIENTNAME>’s organizational or technical infrastructure that impact information assets (e.g., addition of a new network, new hardware/software standard implemented, new method of creating, receiving, maintaining or transmitting data, etc.).
Information Risk Categorization
<CLIENTNAME> shall categorize risks based on business objectives, data sensitivity, threat exposure and vulnerabilities pursuant to information assets.
The categorization process shall take into account the probability and impact of risks materializing and affecting business objectives.
Risks shall be expressed qualitatively as per the risk categorizations defined under the Information Risk Management Framework.
Adequate resources to address risks shall be allocated according to the risk categorization.
Information Risk Treatment
<CLIENTNAME> shall select and implement risk treatment measures to reasonably protect the confidentiality, integrity, availability, secrecy and privacy of information assets. One or more of the following methods shall be used to manage risk:
Risk Reduction: Reducing risk by applying security controls.
Risk Avoidance: Avoiding the activity or condition causing the risk.
Risk Transfer: Transferring the risk to another party.
Risk Retention: Accepting the risk based on the organization’s risk acceptance criteria established in the Information Security Risk Management Framework.
<CLIENTNAME> shall document a risk treatment plan to outline the approach to be used to treat the risks. Such risk treatment plans shall include at a minimum:
Description of information risks.
Appropriate management actions.
Responsibilities and priorities for managing information security risks (risk ownership).
Target dates for implementation of the identified controls.
Risk treatment plans shall be monitored and reviewed periodically as deemed necessary to ensure that risk is managed on a continuous basis.
Selection of Controls
<CLIENTNAME> shall select and implement appropriate controls to mitigate identified risks based on relevance, nature, feasibility and cost effectiveness of the controls.
Under the <CLIENTNAME> Integrated Management System (IMS), controls shall be selected from the list of following standards/frameworks/regulations, inclusive of but not limited to:
ISO 27001.
ISO 27701.
ISO 22301.
UAE Information Assurance (IA) Regulation.
Information Risk Monitoring
<CLIENTNAME> shall continuously review and monitor outcomes of risk assessment and treatment. The monitoring processes shall evaluate:
The effectiveness of security controls.
Changes to information assets and environments of operations.
Compliance with federal and state laws and regulations and applicable standards.
ROLES AND RESPONSIBILITIES
7. POLICY ENFORCEMENT AND EXCEPTIONS
All employees and contractors are responsible for understanding and adhering to this Policy. Any violation of this Policy shall be reported to Policy Owner. Violation of this Policy shall be subjected to disciplinary action by the <CLIENTNAME>'s Human Capital Department, taking into consideration the recommendation of IS Team and in accordance with other applicable policies, laws and regulations.
Disciplinary action may range from a verbal warning to discontinuation of access to facility or services, or termination of employment/contract/agreement, and possible legal action. The actual action taken shall depend on factors such as the seriousness of the breach and the user's disciplinary record.
Any exceptions on the applicability of this Policy should be formally requested through the Policy Exception Management procedure.
MONITORING AND REVIEW
This Policy is developed by <CLIENTNAME> Information Security (IS) Team, owned and reviewed by the CISO. It is approved by the CEO.
<CLIENTNAME> IS Team shall initiate a review of this Policy annually or whenever a significant change occurs in the organization's operating environment.
Feedback, suggestions or questions regarding the Policy shall be directed to the <CLIENTNAME> IS Team at infosec@<clientname>.net.

data.passage_text:
Abstract
INTRODUCTION
<<CLIENT>> and its core business exists in a wider economic environment in which effective relationships with third party suppliers is critical to its continued success. Therefore, it is important that <<CLIENT>>’s relationships with suppliers (Third-Parties) are based on a clear understanding of expectations and requirements in the area of information security. These requirements must be documented and agreed in a manner that leaves no doubt about the importance <<CLIENT>> places on the maintenance of effective security controls to reduce security risks.
PURPOSE
The purpose of this Third-party Security Policy (“Policy”) is to ensure that external stakeholders are compliant with the organization’s security requirements. This Policy defines a framework for third party security and reduces the security risks and liability associated with granting third parties’ access to the organization’s information, information assets and premises.
Further, the Policy establishes a commitment to comply with applicable sections of the federal and global laws and regulations, including but not limited to UAE Information Assurance Regulation, ISO27001 and National Cyber Risk Management Framework (NCRMF).
The following are the key objectives of this Policy:
Restrict third-party access: Establish information security requirements for mitigating the risks associated with each third-party's access to organizational assets.
Establish agreements: Establish agreements that cover all relevant security requirements with third-parties to handle the organization's information assets.
Oversee third-party service delivery: Monitor, review, and audit third-party service delivery.
SCOPE
This Policy applies to all third parties who access or manage information and information assets or use the organization's information and communications technology (ICT) systems.
Any exceptions on the applicability of this Policy should be formally requested through the ‘Policy Exception Management procedure.’
POLICY STATEMENT
<<CLIENT>> shall ensure that all third parties comply with this Policy's information security requirements while providing services that could impact the confidentiality, integrity, availability, secrecy and privacy of the organization’s information and information assets.
POLICY DETAILS
Third-Party Security Approach
<<CLIENT>> shall:
Identify, mandate, and monitor information security and data protection controls processes and procedures to address third-party access to the organization’s information and information assets.
Establish a standardized process and lifecycle for managing third-party relationships, their access to the organization’s information and information assets and the security requirements.
Define the types of obligations applicable to third-parties to protect <<CLIENT>>’s information.
Handle incidents and contingencies associated with third-party access, including the responsibilities of both <<CLIENT>> and its third-parties.
Ensure the availability of the information or information processing provided by either party through resilience, recovery, and contingency arrangements.
Addressing Security and Data Protection Requirements with Third-party Agreements
<<CLIENT>> shall:
Use only third-party service providers providing sufficient guarantees to implement appropriate technical and organizational measures in such a manner that processing will meet the requirements of this Policy.
Define the conditions under which information security and data protection requirements and controls will be documented in an agreement signed by both parties.
Manage the necessary transitions of information, information processing facilities and anything else that needs to be moved and ensuring that information security and data protection is maintained throughout the transition period.
Establish a service management relationship process between <<CLIENT>> and the third-party to:
5.2.4.a Monitor service performance levels to verify adherence to the agreements.
5.2.4.b Review service reports produced by the third-party and arranged regular progress meetings as required by the agreements.
5.2.4.c Conduct audits of third-parties, in conjunction with the review of independent auditor’s reports, if available, and follow-up on issues identified.
Managing Changes to Third-party Services
<<CLIENT>> shall consider the following aspects for information security and data protection review when managing changes to third-party services, at a minimum:
Changes to third-party agreements and/ or sub-contracting to another third-party.
Enhancements to the current services offered.
Development and/ use of new applications, systems, and technologies.
Modifications or updates of the organization’s policies and procedures.
Introduction of new security controls.
Changes and enhancement to technology landscape.
ROLES AND RESPONSIBILITIES
Executive & Senior Management
Make available and support the required resources to drive protection of supplier security risks.
Where relevant, provide direction to and participate in reviews and oversight of supplier security risks.
Admin & Facilities
Work with Procurement and Information Security to ensure that organizational information and information assets are protected from supply chain risks.
Information Security
Work with Procurement to establish:
Security requirements for Third-Party that are to be included in the service delivery agreement for each party.
Requirement(s) for Third-Party to measure and report to the organization on these service requirements.
A mechanism to monitor Third-Party’ services and ensure required delivery reports are received.
Requirements that the information security incidents and problems identified in the reports are appropriately managed.
Requirements for audits to be carried out on supplier services regularly.
Requirement(s) that supplier service agreements include a methodology for communicating change management issues between the organization and the supplier.
Define the parameters for changes that must be communicated between the organization and the supplier.
Requirement(s) to assess the changes taking into account the criticality of business systems and processes involved and re-assess risks.
Procurement
Ensure that the Policy is:
Communicated to each supplier.
Read and formally acknowledged by the supplier.
Maintained, reviewed and updated at planned intervals or if significant changes occur.
Verify that any contract or agreement with Third-Party addresses all aspects of the organization’s information security Policy regarding:
Accessing, processing, communicating or managing the organization’s information or Information systems.
Adding products or services to information systems.
Make sure that proper controls are introduced in the contract in order to verify compliance with the agreed security objectives.
Audit supplier services and infrastructures to verify compliance with agreed security objectives.
Ensure that security requirements for Third-Party are included in the service delivery agreement for each party.
Require Third-Party to measure and report to the organization on these service requirements.
Monitor Third-Party’ services and ensure required delivery reports are received.
Ensure reports received from Third-Party are reviewed by qualified personnel.
Ensure that information security incidents and problems identified in the reports are appropriately managed.
Ensure that audits are carried out on supplier services regularly.
Ensure that supplier service agreements include a methodology for communicating change management issues between the organization and the supplier.
Ensure that defined parameters for changes must be communicated between the organization and the supplier are recorded in supplier service agreements.
Ensure that changes to the service agreement(s) are regularly assessed, considering:
The criticality of business systems and processes involved.
Re-assessment of risks.
Employees, Line Managers and Third Parties (Contractors, Vendors, etc.)
Employees and third parties (including contractors, vendors, etc.) who access organizational premises, information and information assets or use information and communications technology (ICT) systems of the organization are responsible for adhering to this Policy and all its applicable procedures.
Legal
Identify all relevant and applicable laws and regulations to <<CLIENT>> and communicate to the respective parties.
Define standard legal clauses and terms for the contract/agreement to be signed with external parties, employment contracts, confidentiality and Non-Disclosure Agreements (NDAs).
Liaise with IS Team to define and review Information Security clauses in third-party contracts/agreements, employment contracts, confidentiality and Non-Disclosure Agreements (NDAs).
Review third-party vendor, clients and partner contracts and involve IS Team for review of clauses concerning Information Security responsibilities.
POLICY ENFORCEMENT AND EXCEPTIONS
This Policy is to be part of all Employees' and Third Parties' processes, and all are responsible for understanding and adhering to this Policy. Any violation of this Policy shall be reported to Policy Owner. Violation of this Policy shall be subjected to disciplinary action by the <<CLIENT>>'s Human Capital Department, taking into consideration the recommendation of the Information Security department and in accordance with other applicable policies, laws and regulations.
Disciplinary action may range from a verbal warning to discontinuation of access to facility or services, or termination of employment/contract/agreement, and possible legal action. The actual action taken shall depend on factors such as the seriousness of the breach and the user's disciplinary record.
Any exceptions on the applicability of this Policy should be formally requested through the Policy Exception Management procedure.
MONITORING AND REVIEW
This Policy is developed and owned by the <<CLIENT>> Information Security (IS) Team. It is reviewed by the CISO and approved by the CEO.
<<CLIENT>> IS Team will initiate a review and of this Policy annually or whenever a significant change occurs in the organization's operating environment.
Feedback, suggestions or questions regarding the Policy shall be directed to the <<CLIENT>> IS Team at <<Client_ID>>.
